The latter has refused to accept the tariff approved by the Authority in the case of the petitioners. The questions involved in these petitions essentially relate to the scope of the functions and powers of the Power Purchasing Company---The Power Purchasing Company is definitely not empowered to act as a forum of review or appeal in relation to determination of tariff made by the Authority. It is settled law that an act has to be done and performed in the manner prescribed under the law. Reliance placed on PLD 2013 SC 255.--- These petitions are maintainable since they seek judicial review of the conduct of the respondents--- It is settled law that constitutional jurisdiction would be maintainable where rights are based on statute and rules or regulations framed thereunder or when the grievance is in respect of obligations orduty vested in public functionaries or a statutory body. Petitions allowed.","O_UNDERSECTION":"R.2(m), 7(3)(a) \u0026 31---National Electric Power Regulatory Authority Rules, 1998 \u0026 2015, Rr.2(d) \u0026 19 of 2011","O_REMARKS":"-","O_SUBJECT":"ADMINISTRATION OF JUSTICE, MISCELLANEOUS","APPL_IN":"** JGMNT","CASECODE":61068,"PRNTCASECODE":81089,"USERNAME":"UmerRasheedDar"},{"O_ID":6216,"TITLE":"M/s Haq Bahu Sugar Mills Pvt. every word used in a statute must be given its true meaning and the provisions construed together in a harmonious manner--- It is not legal or proper to apply one provisions of law in isolation from the other provision, as surplus ages or redundancy cannot be attributed---  Reliance placed on case law--- Term “Levy” defined--- Question: power and jurisdiction of an officer with the designation of Inland Revenue Officer under sec. 11 of Act, 1990--- Secs 30, 31, 32 of Act, 1990--- Each officer appointed under sec. 30 falls within expression of ‘officer of Inland Revenue’(IRO)--- Power of delegation by board to the officer specified under section 30 to exercise powers and jurisdiction under Subsections 1, 2, 3 \u0026 4 of section 11 of the Act ---  The Board has expressly conferred the powers on the Commissioner and the later delegated the same to IRO--- Subsection 3 of section 30 empowers the Commissioner to assign to the specified officers or class of persons or areas in respect of performance of their functions as may be directed by the latter--- Distinction between expressions “functions” ,“power” \u0026 “jurisdiction”---  Subsection 3 of section 30 does not empower the Commissioner to confer power or jurisdiction--- It is settled law that a delegate cannot further delegate its power unless expressly authorized under the law--- Reliance on 2013 SCMR 1159--- De Smith’s Judicial Review (7th edn)--- Also on 1994 (5) SCC 346--- Order issued by Commissioner Inland Revenue was without lawful authority and jurisdiction --- The Commissioner alone was vested with power and jurisdiction under sec. 11 of Act of 1990 pursuant to Board’s order--- A mere show cause notice is not an adverse order so to justify the exercise of jurisdiction under Art. 199 expect where such notice suffers from want of jurisdiction--- Reliance placed on case law--- Petitions are allowed. ","O_UNDERSECTION":"11 Sales Tax Act 1990 ","O_REMARKS":"-","O_SUBJECT":"INTERPRETATION OF STATUTES, WORDS \u0026 PHRASES, TAX LAWS","APPL_IN":"** JGMNT","CASECODE":48336,"PRNTCASECODE":48336,"USERNAME":"UmerRasheedDar"},{"O_ID":5933,"TITLE":"NEPRA- VS -RTU, NIRC, Islamabad","DDATE":"15-JAN-2015","ATTACHMENTS":"/attachments/judgements/WP-144-2014_______________________________637086448067465181.pdf","ISLANDMARK":0,"O_AFR":1,"O_CITATION":"2015 PLC 148","PARTIES":"NEPRA VS RTU, NIRC, Islamabad","CASENO":"Writ Petition-144-2014","CSNO":144,"BENCHNAME":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","CREATEDDATE":"\/Date(1573030002000)\/","AUTHOR_JUDGES":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","O_IHC_HEADNOTE":"It is mandatory to qualify as an ‘establishment’ or ‘industry’ in order to attract the provisions of industrial Relations Act, 2012.  NEPRA is neither engaged in any business, or production or manufacture of goods, or the provision of services, therefore, in case of NEPRA, Industrial Relations Act, 2012, does not apply.  \r\nRegistration of Respondent no. 2 as trade union by Respondent no. 1 ---Petition is against dismissal of application of NEPRA under section 11 under Industrial Relations (Amendment) Act, 2012 --- Counsel for petitioner: NEPRA is a regulatory authority established under Section 3 of the Regulation of Generation, Transmission and Distribution of Electric Power Act, 1997.\r\nControversy between parties: whether a remedy is available in form of appeal in the Act or not against the order of respondent no. 1 in exercise of powers under section 9 --- Observations, It is mandatory to qualify as an ‘establishment’ or ‘industry’ in order to attract the provisions of industrial relations act, 2012.  NEPRA is neither engaged in any business, or production or manufacture of goods, or the provision of services, therefore, in case of NEPRA, IDR Act does not apply. Regulation of generation, transmission or distribution of electric power is a function within the realm of the administration of the state. Since NEPRA is not an establishment or an industry for the purposes of the Act of 2012, it is, therefore, outside the scope of the\r\napplication of the provisions thereof. The Act of 2012 cannot be extended or invoked and, therefore, a trade union formed by its employees is also not liable to be registered under Section 9 of the Act of 2012. As a corollary it leads to the inevitable conclusion\r\nthat the respondent No. 1 had no jurisdiction relating to the application filed by the employees of NEPRA, let alone proceedings undertaken pursuant thereto by granting the registration and declaring the respondent no. 2 as a Collective Bargaining Agent. And also that no adequate remedy was available to the petitioner therefore, writ petition was maintainable. Even the jurisdictional facts are not immune from scrutiny as was held in 1998 PLC 500. Registrar had no jurisdiction to grant status of union to employees of NEPRA, which was not establishment to attract powers of the Act vesting in registrar various powers.\r\n","O_UNDERSECTION":"Article 199 of the Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan, 1973, 2 of the Industrial Relations Act,20l2 ","O_REMARKS":"-","O_SUBJECT":"NEPRA EMPLOYEE UNION, NIRC","APPL_IN":"** JGMNT","CASECODE":34506,"PRNTCASECODE":34506,"USERNAME":"UmerRasheedDar"},{"O_ID":5930,"TITLE":"Uch Power Pvt Ltd.- VS -FOP, etc.","DDATE":"18-JAN-2016","ATTACHMENTS":"/attachments/judgements/W.P-2384-2015__________________________________637086416469765683.pdf","ISLANDMARK":0,"O_AFR":1,"O_CITATION":"2016 PLD 67 Islamabad","PARTIES":"Uch Power Pvt Ltd. Qureshi","CREATEDDATE":"\/Date(1567577928000)\/","AUTHOR_JUDGES":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","O_IHC_HEADNOTE":"Interpretation of sec 122 of Income Tax Ordinance, 2001--- Show cause notice barred by the limitation provided under the sec--- Even procedural law cannot take away vested or existing rights by applying the limitation period retrospectively, unless such an intention of the legislature had been expressed in unequivocal terms. Reliance was placed on 2015 PTD 424. The said judgment was upheld by the august Supreme Court in Civil Petition no. 1306 of 2014 titled Commissioner of Income Tax, Rawalpindi Zone, RTO Rawalpindi versus Major General (R) Dr. C. M. Anwar etc---Principles and law in context of an amendment made by altering the prescribed period of limitation as enunciated by the Apex Court in 2009 PTD 1392--- Distinction between a charging section and a machinery or procedural provision in context of sec 122  particularly in respect to the limitation having retrospective effect--- Observations, In a case where the assessment order has been passed by the Commissioner, or treated to have been passed by the Commissioner, under section 120 before the amendment was made through the Finance Act, 2009 then the limitation prescribed under the substituted provision will be attracted--- Limitation period as contemplated in sec 122 (2) as amended vide Finance Act 2009 is ‘impregnated with the potential of adding liability of the tax payer is not mere matter of procedure’---Reference decided against the applicant Department.  ","O_UNDERSECTION":"122(2) OF THE INCOME TAX ORDINANCE 2001","O_REMARKS":"-","O_SUBJECT":"INCOME TAX","APPL_IN":"** JGMNT","CASECODE":55705,"PRNTCASECODE":55705,"USERNAME":"UmerRasheedDar"},{"O_ID":3290,"TITLE":"CDA- VS -RM Gulistan Engineer and Contractors Pvt. Minallah","CREATEDDATE":"\/Date(1567272713000)\/","AUTHOR_JUDGES":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","O_IHC_HEADNOTE":"(a) The Code of Criminal Procedure, 1898--- Criminal Appeal.\r\n\r\n(b) Deposition of solitary witness--- Principle--- It is settled law that a conviction can be handed down on the basis of the deposition of even a solitary witness and that quality of evidence and not quantity is required to be considered by a trial court--- It depends upon the facts and circumstances in each particular case whether a solitary witness should be believed or not.\r\n\r\nRiaz Hussain v The State (2001 SCMR 177); Allah Bakhsh v. Shammi and others (PLD 1980 SC 225). Rel. \r\n\r\nMuhammad Siddique alias Ashraf alias Achhi and 3 others v. The State. (1971 SCMR 659). Ref.\r\n\r\n(c) Testimony of abductee/ kidnapee--- In cases involving the commission of the offence of kidnapping or abduction, unless there were strong reasons to discredit the testimony of the abductee/ kidnapee, the latter’s statement carries substantial evidentiary value. \r\n\r\nThe State v. Nazir Ahmad (1999 SCMR 610). Rel \u0026 Foll. \r\n\r\n(d) Anti Terrorism Act, 1997, S. 7--- Ingredients of the offence.\r\n\r\nShahbaz Khan alias Tippu and others v Special Judge Anti- Terrorism Court No. 3, Lahore and others (PLD 2016 SC 1), Malik Muhammad Mumtaz Qadri v. The State and others (PLD 2016 SC 17), Khuda-e- Noor versus The State (PLD 2016 SC 195), Kashif Ali versus The Judge, Anti –Terrorism, Court No. II, Lahore and others. (PLD 2016 SC 951) \u0026 Waris Ali and 5 others v The State (2017 SCMR 1572). Ref.\r\n\r\nAppeals against convictions and sentences under Anti Terrorism Act, 1997 were allowed while convictions and sentences under S. 365-A of the Pakistan Penal Code, 1860, were upheld.\r\n","O_UNDERSECTION":"365-A Anti-Terrorism Act (XXVII of 1997) ATA","O_REMARKS":"-","O_SUBJECT":"Anti-Terrorism Act","APPL_IN":"** JGMNT","CASECODE":9735,"PRNTCASECODE":9735,"USERNAME":"UmerRasheedDar"},{"O_ID":3234,"TITLE":"Flight Lt. Sec 7 (3) (d)--- Filing of appeal under the section of the said Ordinance challenging the orders of FPSC. \r\n\r\n(b) Criteria for induction of Armed Forces officers in Civil Service---The criteria is based on three factors: Firstly, merit; secondly, provincial/ regional quota; and lastly, the formula which was referred to in the minutes of the meeting dated 10-09-1991 subjected to confirmation /approval of the Government of Pakistan through the Secretary Establishment and in consultation with Ministry of Defence.\r\n \r\nESTA Code, SI no. 244--- Methodology of the induction of officers of the armed forces\r\n\r\n(d) Illegal Formula enshrined in sub- para 4 of para 10 of the above mentioned minutes--- Supreme Court’s detailed reasoning in Civil Appeal No. 6-K of 2017 left only two factors i.e., merit and regional/ provincial quota. \r\n\r\nIn the light of the said judgment of Supreme Court, the Appellant qualifies to be inducted in PSP. \r\n\r\nAppeal was allowed.   \r\n","O_UNDERSECTION":"7(3)(d) Occupational Groups and Services Rules, 1990","O_REMARKS":"-","O_SUBJECT":"Nomination of officers of Armed Forces in the Civil Service of Pakistan","APPL_IN":"** induction in the ‘Secretariat Group’ benefits of BPS 21 under the Time Scale Formula and his further promotion to BPS under policy relating to the grant of higher pay scale on a meritorious basis--- Absorption/ Induction in the Secretariat Group--- Petitioner’s case did not fall under the modes prescribed for induction as an officer in the Secretariat Group--- Benefit under time scale formula--- Time scale formula is not prescribed under the Act of 1973 nor the rules made there under--- It is granted to specific categories of professions through policy approved by the competent authority and with the concurrence of Finance Division--- Promotion on basis of meritorious services--- Criteria--- High Court under Art 199 could not assume the role of the Special Selection Committee so as to assess the contributions of an officer--- Also, it is settled law that a thing ought to be done in the manner prescribed under the law—Reliance placed on PLD 2013 SC 255--- ICAs succeeded. Pakistan, 1973, Art. 199--- the Land Reforms Act, 1977, Ss. 7 , 9 \u0026 15--- Illegality of land possessed by respondent no. 2 ---Declaration of owning or possessing land---Disposal of excess land by Government--- Allotment of land in favour of other persons--- Declaring the disposal null and void--- Predecessors in interest seeking redetermination/reclassification of the units---Dismissal of applications--- Chief Land Commissioner/ Administrative, ICT upholding the dismissal for reclassification of resumed land--- Lahore High Court, Rawalpindi Bench remitting the matter for reconsideration and decision afresh --- Order passed by Chief Commissioner, ICT--- Challenging the order before the Lahore High Court, Rawalpindi Bench--- Re-remanding the matter--- Chief Commissioner re- dismissed the claims. \r\n\r\n(b) The Land Reforms Act, 1977--- Protracted litigation--- Finality of orders--- Predecessors in interest accepted the orders--- Option of getting long standing entries corrected, not exercised--- Without setting aside the proceedings under the Act of 1977 and the orders passed therein, the predecessors in interest had virtually attempted to indirectly annul and render the proceedings under the Act ineffective by seeking a roving enquiry and that too after more than six years since the orders. \r\n(c) Maxim--- It is settled law that what cannot be done directly can also not be done indirectly.\r\n\r\n(d) The Land Reforms Act, 1977--- Legislative intent--- Purpose and object--- Predecessors in interest were stopped on account of their won conduct from challenging the proceedings under the Act of 1977.\r\n\r\n Petition dismissed.\r\n","O_UNDERSECTION":"4, 7 \u0026 24 Land Reforms Act (II of 1977)","O_REMARKS":"-","O_SUBJECT":"ALLOTMENT OF Pakistan Ltd., etc.- VS -NEPRA, etc.","DDATE":"22-JUN-2016","ATTACHMENTS":"/attachments/judgements/WP-4178-2015________________636975037396906615.pdf","ISLANDMARK":0,"O_AFR":1,"O_CITATION":"2016 CLC 1805","PARTIES":"High Flying Solar Development Pakistan Ltd., etc. VS NEPRA, etc.","CASENO":"Writ Petition-4178-2015","CSNO":4178,"BENCHNAME":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","CREATEDDATE":"\/Date(1561890202000)\/","AUTHOR_JUDGES":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","O_IHC_HEADNOTE":"Audi alteram partem, doctrine of---Applicability---Determining factors---Principles of natural justice are flexible and not rigid and determination of application of these principles depends on circumstances of each case---Various factors may be taken into consideration for such purpose, such as the nature of inquiry, the subject matter being dealt with, whether anything unfair can be inferred if opportunity is not afforded, whether there is no apprehension of injustice etc.---Depending on facts and circumstances of each case, it would be sufficient if ‘elementary and essential principles of fairness’ have been fulfilled---In a given situation, it may be sufficient if person affected has been made aware of the nature of allegations, has been afforded a fair and reasonable opportunity to defend allegations and to controvert any statement made against him or her---It would not be mandatory in every case to examine witnesses in presence of person against whom allegations have been made, or to afford him or her an opportunity for cross examination---If a person who has been afforded a fair opportunity, which satisfies the requirements of elementary and essential principles of fairness, does not appear or fails to avail opportunity or is otherwise defiant, then he or she may not be able to raise a grievance relating to violation of principles of natural justice, as they would have no application in the given circumstances---In the exceptional cases application of doctrine of ‘audi alteram partem’ may even be excluded.\r\n\r\n            The University of Dacca through its Vice Chancellor and another v. Zakir Ahmed PLD 1965 SC 90 and Justice Khurshid Anwar Bhinder v. Federation of Pakistan PLD 2010 SC 483 rel.\r\n\r\nRegulation of Generation, Transmission and Distribution of Electric Power Act (XL of 1997)--- Ss.6 \u0026 7---National Electric Power Regulatory Authority Upfront (Approval and Procedure) Regulations, 2011, Regln.4(8)---Power Generation Policy, 2006---Constitution of Pakistan, Art.10-A---Audi alteram partem, doctrine of---Petitioners were power generation companies and were aggrieved of dismissal of their applications regarding determination of electricity tariff by authorities---Validity---Proceedings before National Electric Power Regulatory Authority must reflect its independence and autonomy which included ensuring transparent proceedings and affording opportunity of meaningful hearings to stakeholders before passing an order, determination or decision---National Electric Power Regulatory Authority was to strictly follow and implement regulations of National Electric Power Regulatory Authority Upfront (Approval and Procedure) Regulations, 2011, particularly Regln.4(8) thereof---High Court declared rejection of applications without affording an opportunity of hearing or recording of reasons reflecting substance of arguments advanced before National Electric Power Regulatory Authority was ultra vires the Regulation of Generation, Transmission and Distribution of Electric Power Act, 1997, read with National Electric Power Regulatory Authority Upfront (Approval and Procedure) Regulations, 2011, in violation of fundamental right of due process guaranteed under Art.10-A of the Constitution and the same was without lawful authority and jurisdiction---High Court set aside the order passed by authorities and remanded the matter to authorities for decision on application afresh---Constitutional petition was allowed accordingly.\r\n                    Muhammad Yasin v. FOP through Secretary, Establishment Division, Islamabad and others PLD 2012 SC 132; Flying Board and Paper Products Ltd. v. Government of Pakistan and others 2010 SCMR 517; Lahore Development Authority through D.G. and others v. Ms Imran Tiwana and others 2015 SCMR 1739; In Re: Poyser and Mills Arbitration [1964] 2 Q.B 467 and Maqbool Ahmed and others v. passed by Review Board constituted/ established under sec. 12 of the Sacked Employees Act, 2010--- Questions need to be considered: a) the scope of interference in respect of orders passed by the Review Board in the light of sub section 8 of sec. 13 b) whether the Review Board was vested with jurisdiction to consider and decide the petitions filed by the respondents c) whether in the absence of a written order the Review Board could have assumed jurisdiction and lastly d) whether the Review Board was vested with power and jurisdiction to decide the cases of the respective respondents. --- Scheme of the Sacked Emplyees Act --- Categories of those who are vested with statutory right to file a petition before the Review Board--- Establishment of Review Board under the Act--- the Act is a special law enacted as a beneficial legislation for reinstatement of employees defined --- The composition of the board is such that virtually the decision making powers vests in the Federal Government--- Examining the scope of interference in the decisions and orders of the Review Board in exercise of jurisdiction vested in this Court--- Ouster clause --- Principles: PLD 1958 SC 104; PLD 1960 SC 113; PLD 1963 SC 191; (1) 67 I A 222;  PLD 1969 SC 241; PLD 1989 SC 26; 1991 SCMR 599; PLD 1997 SC 3; 2004 SCMR 440;  PLD 2011 SC 407; PLD 1994 SC 738; PLJ 2006 SC 1422; --- Halbury’s Law of England--- Anismic case ([1969] 1 All E.R. 208)--- Re Gilmore’s Application [1957] 1 ALL E.R. 796--- In the light of principles, the scheme of the statute clearly shows that the Legislature has clearly and manifestly declared orders and decisions of the Review Board to be “final” and that they cannot be called into question in any court, authority or Tribunal--- Beneficial legislation needs beneficial and liberal construction--- Reliance placed on case law--- Petitions were dismissed.","O_UNDERSECTION":"5, 11, 12, 13 of the Sacked Employees (Re-instatement) Act, 2010","O_REMARKS":"-","O_SUBJECT":"INTERPRETATION OF STATUTES, JURISDICTION, MISCELLANEOUS","APPL_IN":"** VS Competition Commission of Pakistan, etc.","CASENO":"Writ Petition-1987-2015","CSNO":1987,"BENCHNAME":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","CREATEDDATE":"\/Date(1561889918000)\/","AUTHOR_JUDGES":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","O_IHC_HEADNOTE":"Petitioners challenged notices issued by Commission requiring the petitioners to submit information --- Competition Commission Act, 2010--- Scheme and relevant provisions--- Expression “purposes of the Act” are enumerated in Chapter-II read with sections 28, 29 and 31--- Powers of the commission--- Sec 37 refers to ‘inquiries’ \u0026 ‘studies’ --- It is settled law that authorities vested with powers under a statute cannot use or exercise powers for the purposes of making indiscriminate, roving and fishing enquiries--- Reliance placed on PLD 1992 SC 485 (Assistant Director, Intelligence and Investigation, Karachi versus M/s B.R. Herman and others)---  Also Dr. Akhtar Hassan Khan versus Federation of Pakistan (2012 SCMR 455)--- Nor should any enquiry be launched upon merely on the basis of vague and indefinite allegations nor should the person alleging malafide be allowed a roving enquiry into the files of the government for the purposes of fishing out some kind of a case--- Also Wazirzada versus Chief of Air Staff, PAF (1998 SCMR 1579) --- Petitions were allowed.  ","O_UNDERSECTION":"28, 29, 32, 36, 37 Competition Act (XIX of 2010)","O_REMARKS":"-","O_SUBJECT":"ADMINISTRATION OF JUSTICE, MISCELLANEOUS","APPL_IN":"** mentioned in an advertisement published in Press, cannot be ignored, altered or deviated from during the selection process.","O_UNDERSECTION":"24, 31 Allama Iqbal Open University Act (XXXIX of 1974), Adv)","DDATE":"07-JUN-2016","ATTACHMENTS":"/attachments/judgements/WP-1660-2015________________636975032110837330.pdf","ISLANDMARK":0,"O_AFR":1,"O_CITATION":"2016 PLC(CS) 1305","PARTIES":"Naseem Iqbal VS FOP, etc.              (Syed Buland Sohail Adv)","CASENO":"Writ Petition-1660-2015","CSNO":1660,"BENCHNAME":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","CREATEDDATE":"\/Date(1561889673000)\/","AUTHOR_JUDGES":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","O_IHC_HEADNOTE":"Challenging the appointment of respondent no. 2 as Commissioner and then Chairman of SECP--- Petitioners contended that a Commissioner served for two terms cannot be appointed for a third term according to SECP Act, 1997; They further placed reliance on Bank of Punjab and another versus Haris Steel Industries (Pvt) Ltd and others (PLD 2010 SC 1109)--- Respondents argued that there is no bar for such appointment and achievements of such respondent has been widely acknowledged--- Scheme and the relevant provisions of the Act of 1997 was examined--- Establishment of Commission, sec 3, SECP Act, 1997--- Defining ‘Chairman’ as in clause (d) of sec 2--- Composition of Commission, Sec. 5, SECP Act, 1997--- It is implicit in the language used by the legislature in sec. 6 that though a person could be appointed as a Commissioner, despite having held the position for two consecutive terms, but the appointment as a chairman would be barred. The commissioner, therefore, can be reappointed for a second term--- Distinction between ‘reappointment’ \u0026 ‘appointment’ --- If a commissioner is either not reappointed upon expiry of the initial term, or the cumulative period pursuant to reappointment for a second term has ended, then in both the eventualities the vacancy is to be filled by the appointment of a qualified person. Disticntion--- Reliance on 2013 SCMR 1159 (Muhammad Ashraf Tiwana and others versus Pakistan and others)--- The expression ‘re-appointed’ a Commissioner would become redundant and lose its significance if it was to be interpreted at par with an ‘appointment’ and thus made subject to a fresh process elucidated in judgment of above referred case--- Reading of sections 5, 6 \u0026 7 shows that there is no clog on a person who has ceased to hold the office of a Commissioner to participate in the process initiated to fill the vacancy and to be appointed if found to be the most qualified form amongst the eligible candidates--- A commissioner cannot be reappointed after the respective cumulative periods of two terms has ended, while there is no bar if such a person is appointed pursuant to participating in the process for selection of the most qualified person in the light of the principles and law elucidated in the judgment of Tiwana’s case--- Petition without merit.","O_UNDERSECTION":"3, 5, 6, 7, 8, 12, 18 Securities and Exchange Commission of Pakistan Act (XLII of 1997)","O_REMARKS":"-","O_SUBJECT":"INTERPRETATION OF STATUTES, APPOINTMENT","APPL_IN":"** etc.","DDATE":"13-APR-2015","ATTACHMENTS":"/attachments/judgements/WP-1030-2015__________________636975030761434960.pdf","ISLANDMARK":0,"O_AFR":1,"O_CITATION":"2015 PLD 156 Islamabad","PARTIES":"Syed Zafar Ali Shah VS FOP, etc.","CASENO":"Writ Petition-1030-2015","CSNO":1030,"BENCHNAME":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Aamer Farooq, Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","CREATEDDATE":"\/Date(1561889538000)\/","AUTHOR_JUDGES":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","O_IHC_HEADNOTE":"Arts. 64 \u0026 199--- Question: If members tender their resignation to Speaker later must accept the resignation--- Speaker must take into account three elements: 1) Resignation was tendered voluntarily 2) It was genuine 3) With actual intention to relinquish--- Satisfaction of Speaker in refusing to accept resignation cannot be subjected to Art. 199--- Onus of prove on candidate to concerned seat is meant to prove the allegations--- Sanctity attached to elections based on principle that will of people is sacrosanct--- Political questions: Judicial restraint in such matters is not absolute rule--- Trichotomy of powers.  ","O_UNDERSECTION":"Arts. 64 \u0026 199 Constitution of Pakistan","O_REMARKS":"-","O_SUBJECT":"CONSTITUTION OF PAKISTAN 1973, ELECTION","APPL_IN":"** JGMNT","CASECODE":49080,"PRNTCASECODE":49080,"USERNAME":"UmerRasheedDar"},{"O_ID":1953,"TITLE":"Dewan Petroleum Pvt Ltd- VS -Execution Directior SECP","DDATE":"25-JAN-2017","ATTACHMENTS":"/attachments/judgements/SECP-APP-05-2015_________________636975028821415553.pdf","ISLANDMARK":0,"O_AFR":1,"O_CITATION":"2017 CLD 1237","PARTIES":"Dewan Petroleum Pvt Ltd VS Execution Directior SECP","CASENO":"SECP Appeal-5-2015","CSNO":5,"BENCHNAME":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","CREATEDDATE":"\/Date(1561889344000)\/","AUTHOR_JUDGES":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","O_IHC_HEADNOTE":"Appellate company was aggrieved of transfer of share to natural person by respondent company--- Through this petition filed under section 34 of the SECP Act, 1997, appellant company assailed order of appellate bench of SECP whereby the latter passed restraining order against the appellant company to hold AGM--- Questions: Whether an appeal against an interim order is competent under sec. 34 of the Act of 1997 and if the appeal is competent, then whether the impugned order is sustainable in law and lastly, whether the two constitutional petitions filed by the share holders are maintainable---  Sec 34 of the Act of 1997--- Filing of an appeal to the Court i.e. a High Court in respect of ‘an order ’ of the Commission or the Appellate Bench or an order made under sub sec. (2) of sec 32 (b)--- Right of appeal against an “order”----Defining the expression ‘order’--- Black Law’s Dictionary; Oxford Advanced Learner’s Dictionary; --- the words ‘an order’ by no stretch of imaginations can be construed as meaning a final order or decision which disposes of or decides the appeal---   Where the legislature had intended that a remedy against an interim order was not available then the same was expressly barred by using express words to this effect--- Provisions have to be construed by reading them as a whole and in a harmonious manner--- It is the duty of a court to construe the provisions of a legislative enactment so that it is in consonance with what the legislature had intended--- Every word must be given meaning and effect since redundancy cannot be imputed to the legislature--- sec 34 (1) of the Act of 1997 provides for the scope of the right of appeal while sub sec (2) prescribes the period during which the said right can be exercised--- The legislature, in its wisdom, has used words in relation to the right of appeal which cannot be construed as excluding an interim order or circumscribing it to a final order or decision--- The appeal was held to be competent--- Question of maintainability --- Right of appeal already present under sec. 33 of the Act of 1997--- It is settled law that a High Court will not entertain a petition under Art. 199 when another appropriate remedy is available, is not a rule of law barring the jurisdiction of the Court--- [1999 SCMR 138]; [1999 SCMR 1881]; [PLD 1996 SC 246] 1993 SCMR 1108; PLD 1992 SC 847; PLD 1972 SC 279; PLD 1961 SC 119; PLD 1958 SC 437--- Petitions were held not to be competent nor could the right of appeal have been bypassed--- Legality of order discussed --- There is no cavil that statutory provisions can either be treated as ‘directory’ or ‘mandatory’. It is now settled law that a statute is understood to be ‘directory’ when it contains matters merely of directions but not when those directions are followed by an express provision that in default of following them legal consequences would ensue. If consequences are provided for a default then the provisions are necessarily to be construed as ‘mandatory’--- Reference placed on PLD 1974 SC 134; 2001 SCMR 1001; 2011 SCMR 420; 2000 SCMR 1305; PLD 2007 SC 362; PLD 2010 SC 759--- Provisions of sec 158 of the Companies Ordinance 1984 and the timelines provided therein are definitely ‘mandatory’ and not merely ‘directory’--- There is no power vested either in the Commission or the learned Appellate Bench to pass an order which has the effect of suspending a statutory provision--- The scheme of the Ordinance, 1983 does not envisage passing an open ended blanket injunctive order whereby a company, an incorporated entity, could be restrained from holding an AGM under sec. 158 of the Ordinance--- The impugned order was therefore held to be without lawful authority, jurisdiction and in violation of the mandatory provisions of the Ordinance along with Act of 1997--- Provisions of sec 158 are mandatory particularly the timelines prescribed therein,. Appeal was allowed and impugned order was set aside","O_UNDERSECTION":"Art. 199 Constitution of Pakistan, 78A, 160A, 33(i)(d), 34, 485(1), 170, 180, 158 of the Companies Ordinance, 1984 ","O_REMARKS":"-","O_SUBJECT":"COMPANIES LAWS, CONSTITUTION OF PAKISTAN 1973, INTERPRETATION OF STATUTES","APPL_IN":"** JGMNT","CASECODE":49957,"PRNTCASECODE":47381,"USERNAME":"UmerRasheedDar"},{"O_ID":1952,"TITLE":"FOP etc- VS -Asad Javed etc          (Ch. Naseem Sikandar Adv, Ahmer Bilal Sufi Adv, Amicus)","CASENO":"Intra Court Appeal-249-2015","CSNO":249,"BENCHNAME":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah, Former Honourable Mr. Justice Shaukat Aziz Siddiqui","CREATEDDATE":"\/Date(1561889169000)\/","AUTHOR_JUDGES":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","O_IHC_HEADNOTE":"Appeal against the judgment to the extent of declaring that the petitioner was entitled to remissions allowed for the entire period of his sentence--- Transfer of Offenders Ordinance, 2002--- Agreement between UK \u0026 Pakistan on transfer of Prisoners resulted into Ordinance in the year 2002--- Respondent was transferred to the Country to serve the remaining sentence of 18 years 11 months and 2 days--- He was released but latter got arrested on another charge and was released--- On early release, Lord Chancellor \u0026 Secretary of State for Justice of UK protested vide correspondence whereupon respondent was against taken into custody---  Respondent later on sought his release and remission in sentence--- Questions arose, interpretation of provisions of Ordinance with Agreement in context of section 9 (4) ans section 12; secondly, the interpretation of phrase court of competent jurisdiction in Pakistan as referred to in section 9 (4) of the Ordinance, thirdly, whether in the facts and circumstances of the instant cas---\r\nInstant appeal allowed. \r\n","O_UNDERSECTION":"2(c), 3(2), 4, 5, 6, 7, 9(4) \u0026 12 Transfer of Offenders Ordinance (XXXVII of 2002)","O_REMARKS":"-","O_SUBJECT":"CONSTITUTION OF PAKISTAN 1973, ADMINISTRATION OF JUSTICE, MISCELLANEOUS","APPL_IN":"** JGMNT","CASECODE":50492,"PRNTCASECODE":50492,"USERNAME":"UmerRasheedDar"},{"O_ID":1951,"TITLE":"Pakistan Stone Development Company Ltd.- VS -Muhammad Yousaf, etc.","DDATE":"16-JAN-2018","ATTACHMENTS":"/attachments/judgements/FAO-90-2015____________636975025431373599.pdf","ISLANDMARK":0,"O_AFR":1,"O_CITATION":"2018 CLC 877","PARTIES":"Pakistan Stone Development Company Ltd. Qureshi","CREATEDDATE":"\/Date(1561888833000)\/","AUTHOR_JUDGES":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","O_IHC_HEADNOTE":"Appeal filed against dismissal of application that had been filed under Order XXI Rule 89 of CPC--- Observations, Rules 89 \u0026 90 of Order XXI CPC--- Reliance on Supreme Court judgment dated 03- 11- 2015 passed in Civil Review Petition no. 383/ 2005 in Civil Appeal no. 670/ 2002 titled as Zakria Ghani and 4 others versus Muhammad Ikhlaq Memon and 8 others--- It has been elaborated and held that failing to comply with Order XXI Rule 90 or 89 CPC has an inevitable consequence, that the judgment debtor is precluded from making any challenge to the validity of sale at a subsequent stage--- If a judgment debtor chooses not to take advantage of the opportunities afforded to him by the law the matter comes to an end--- supreme Court in the said judgment also placed reliance on PLD 1987 SC 512 to signify the sanctity and stability of judicial sales--- The time period for making an application under either of Rules is 30 days from the date of sale and the limitation prescribed under Art. 166 of the Limitation Act shall be attracted--- it has been held by the Apex Court that ‘if a judgment debtor chooses not to take advantage of the opportunities afforded to him by the law the matter comes to an end.’ --- Observations, Argument that appellants could file an application under Order XXI Rule 89 at any stage is misconceived--- it is an admitted position that the appellants had not filed any application under Order Xxi Rule 89 CPC within the prescribed period of limitation--- Held, application filed by the appellants under Order XXI Rule 89 was time barred and therefore, incompetent--- Appeal stood dismissed.   ","O_UNDERSECTION":"S. 19, 66, 89, 90, Civil Procedure Code (V of 1908), 89 \u0026 92---Limitation Act (IX of 1908), ","O_REMARKS":"-","O_SUBJECT":"Banking","APPL_IN":"** JGMNT","CASECODE":54905,"PRNTCASECODE":54905,"USERNAME":"UmerRasheedDar"},{"O_ID":1949,"TITLE":"M/s Al Haj- VS -Collector of Customs                     (Asad Raza, Advocate)","DDATE":"20-APR-2016","ATTACHMENTS":"/attachments/judgements/Custom.Ref-31-2015_____________636975021810139238.pdf","ISLANDMARK":0,"O_AFR":1,"O_CITATION":"2016 PTD 2146","PARTIES":"M/s Al Haj VS Collector of Customs                     (Asad Raza, Advocate)","CASENO":"Custom Reference-31-2015","CSNO":31,"BENCHNAME":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah, Former Honourable Mr. Justice Noor-Ul-Haq N. Qureshi","CREATEDDATE":"\/Date(1561888643000)\/","AUTHOR_JUDGES":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","O_IHC_HEADNOTE":"Customs Act 1969 \u0026 Rules of 2001 --- Duties and taxes chargeable on the petroleum products transported by the applicant have neither been levied nor paid--- Chapter XXII of the Rules of 2001 exclusively deals with the transport of POL products to Afghanistan and, inter alia, prescribes the eligibility criteria for the granting of a license or permit--- The object and purpose of granting a license or permit under the Rules of 2001 is altogether different from a license issued under section 12 or 13 of the Act of 1969--- Rules 557- 569, when read together, clearly show that an exhaustive procedure has been prescribed in the case of transport of petroleum products to Afghanistan--- It is the obligation of the carrier to inform the collector of origination regarding the endorsement made on behalf of the ISAF at the time the products are delivered--- Variation of one percent in the quantity declared in the permit, therefore, becomes the liability of the carrier--- Show- cause notice merely informed the applicant regarding the variation of more than one percent and the same has not been disputed at any stage by the applicant--- Sec 194- B of Customs Act, 1969---Question whether the limitation for passing an order within the specified time is mandatory or directory? ---It has been consistently held by the august Supreme Court that the determination of whether a provision is mandatory or directory largely depends upon the intention and language in which the provision is couched. Reliance placed on 2014 SCMR 45; 2000 SCMR 1305; 2001 SCMR 1001; PLD 2010 SC 759---No consequences have been provided for deciding an appeal beyond the time prescribed in the first proviso, as has been reproduced--- Provision was held to be directory in nature.    ","O_UNDERSECTION":"12, 13, 32, 110, 193 \u0026 194-B(1)---Customs Rules, 2001, Rr. 558, 559, 560, 561, 564, 566 \u0026 569, Customs Act (IV of 1969)","O_REMARKS":"-","O_SUBJECT":"INTERPRETATION OF STATUTES, TAX LAWS","APPL_IN":"** JGMNT","CASECODE":53092,"PRNTCASECODE":53092,"USERNAME":"UmerRasheedDar"},{"O_ID":1948,"TITLE":"Dewan Salman Fibre Ltd.- VS -Dewan Petroleum Pvt Ltd.","DDATE":"15-MAR-2016","ATTACHMENTS":"/attachments/judgements/CO-07-2015_______________636975019095110469.pdf","ISLANDMARK":0,"O_AFR":1,"O_CITATION":"2016 CLD 1049","PARTIES":"Dewan Salman Fibre Ltd. others","DDATE":"11-JAN-2017","ATTACHMENTS":"/attachments/judgements/ICA-510-2014_______________636975006014487495.pdf","ISLANDMARK":0,"O_AFR":1,"O_CITATION":"2017 PLC(CS)N 58","PARTIES":"Chairman FBR, Islamabad VS Atta Muhammad Masood \u0026 others","CASENO":"Intra Court Appeal-510-2014","CSNO":510,"BENCHNAME":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","CREATEDDATE":"\/Date(1561887064000)\/","AUTHOR_JUDGES":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","O_IHC_HEADNOTE":"Writs filed by respondents had been allowed for upgrading their posts --- Observations, Questions emerged for consideration, firstly, whether up-gradation falls within the ambit of the terms and conditions of service; secondly, under what provision of law is a post upgraded; thirdly, if upgrading a post is a matter of policy then the scope and conditions thereof; fourthly, whether a right exists for an employee to claim that the post held by him or her be upgraded; fifth, whether a High Court exercising jurisdiction under Art. 199 may direct that process be initiated for upgrading a post, and lastly, whether up grading posts in the same department or other departments can be treated as a valid ground for raising a plea of discrimination in context of Art. 25--- It is an admitted position that upgrading a post is not envisaged or provided for in any statute, nor is it one of the terms and conditions of service--- A policy has been formulated for upgrading a post--- An employee is entitled to claim upgradation of a post on a personal basis if he is able to show that the conditions mentioned in clause (d) of para 2 of policy are fulfilled---A constitutional petition seeking a writ of mandamus would only be competent if the pre-requisites have been complied with --- Reliance placed on District Magistrate, Lahore and another versus Syed Raza Karim [PLD 1961 SC 178]--- It is essential for a person who invokes the jurisdiction under Art 199, seeking a writ in the nature of mandamus, to explicitly show that there exists a legal right vested in such a person to insist upon a clear duty being performed by some public officer in respect of that right--- Moreover, that a demand for performance of such duty ought to have been made and the same had been met with refusal by the public officer or authority in relation to the right being asserted. A person seeking a writ of mandamus is further required to satisfy the Court that no other equally expeditious, in expensive and efficacious remedy is available to him or her--- The expression ‘upgradation’ explained--- Reliance placed on Regional Commissioner Income Tax, Northern Region, Islamabad and another versus Syed Munawar Ali and others [2016 SCMR 859]--- Upgradation of a post simpliciter does not confer a right to the person holding such an upgraded post to claim the benefits as well. No right can be claimed for the benefits, such as higher pay, allowances, perks and privileges and pensionary benefits due to up gradation of the post, since the same can only be conferred on an employee pursuant to promotion--- Reliance placed on Lt. Col. (R.) Abdul Wajid Malik versus Government of the Punjab and another [2007 PLC (CS) 617]--- Also, that discrimination cannot be raised as a valid plea in case of up gradation of a post nor upgradation of a post was a vested right. Reliance placed on Government of Pakistan M/o Railways, through Secretary and others v. Jamshed Hussain Cheema and others [2016 SCMR 442]---  Reference also made to  Chief Commissioner, Inland Revenue and another versus Muhammad Afzal Khan and others [2014 PLC (CS) 829] \u0026 Ali Azhar Khan Baloch and others versus Province of Sindh and others [2015 SCMR 456]---  The principles and law enunciated by the August Supreme Court highlighted--- In the light of principles, no right could be shown to exist in favour of the respondents to claim that the posts held by them be up graded--- Appeals against the upgradation of the respondents were allowed. ","O_UNDERSECTION":"2, 3, 11, 15, 23 Pakistan Medical and Dental Council Ordinance (XXXII of 1962), Art. 199 Constitution of Pakistan, 21 of the General Clauses Act, 1897 ","O_REMARKS":"-","O_SUBJECT":"Pakistan Medical and Dental Council , Qanun-e-Shahadat , Estoppel","APPL_IN":"** JGMNT","CASECODE":32282,"PRNTCASECODE":32282,"USERNAME":"UmerRasheedDar"},{"O_ID":1773,"TITLE":"Muhammad Azram- VS -NIH etc","DDATE":"15-OCT-2014","ATTACHMENTS":"/attachments/judgements/WP-178-2013_________________636971732324869563.pdf","ISLANDMARK":0,"O_AFR":1,"O_CITATION":"2015 PLC(CS) 537","PARTIES":"Muhammad Azram VS NIH etc","CASENO":"Writ Petition-178-2013","CSNO":178,"BENCHNAME":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","CREATEDDATE":"\/Date(1561559695000)\/","AUTHOR_JUDGES":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","O_IHC_HEADNOTE":"By now it is a settled law that disciplinary proceedings and Criminal proceedings are distinguishable ---The Court, weighs various factors in deciding, on the facts and circumstances of the case, particularly on the touchstone of equitable principles, whether to exercise the powers and jurisdiction when there is a delay, and whether the discretionary relief will be granted or refused.\r\nSecurity Guard had case registered against him upon which he got dismissed without disciplinary proceedings --- He got acquitted --- His request for re-instatement was regretted without proper hearing ---Having no other remedy available, petitioner invoked writ jurisdiction --- Observations, NIH Regulations --- services could not have been terminated without observing principles of natural justice, The august Supreme Court has consistently held that in cases where the giving of notice is a necessary condition, the failure to do so renders the order void --- Can criminal and disciplinary proceedings synonymous? --- By now it is a settled law that disciplinary proceedings and Criminal proceedings are distinguishable.  The fact that the petitioner had been dismissed while the other employee of the same institution who had been re-instated  produced an acquittal order before such an order could be passed against him has no relevance for the purposes of determining whether they were similarly Situated ---on the facts and circumstances of the present case, the petitioner is entitled to the grant of discretionary relief under Article 199 of the Constitution and laches will not operate as a bar, s an equitable relief, jurisdiction under Article 199 is subject to the bar known as laches. The Court, nevertheless, weighs various factors in deciding, on the facts and circumstances of the case, particularly on the touchstone of equitable principles, whether to exercise the powers and jurisdiction when there is a delay, and whether the discretionary relief will be granted or refused.","O_UNDERSECTION":"24, 63, 69 National Institute of Health Employees (Service) Regulations, 1989, 199, 10-A \u0026 25 Constitution of Pakistan","O_REMARKS":"-","O_SUBJECT":"CONSTITUTION OF PAKISTAN 1973, ADMINISTRATION OF JUSTICE, PENALTIES","APPL_IN":"** JGMNT","CASECODE":22862,"PRNTCASECODE":22862,"USERNAME":"UmerRasheedDar"},{"O_ID":1772,"TITLE":"INFOSPAN Pvt Ltd- VS -M/s Shaheen Foundation etc","DDATE":"13-APR-2018","ATTACHMENTS":"/attachments/judgements/FAO-102-2013______________636971720194132256.pdf","ISLANDMARK":0,"O_AFR":1,"O_CITATION":"2019 CLC 750","PARTIES":"INFOSPAN Pvt Ltd VS M/s Shaheen Foundation etc","CASENO":"First Appeal Against Order-102-2013","CSNO":102,"BENCHNAME":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","CREATEDDATE":"\/Date(1561558482000)\/","AUTHOR_JUDGES":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","O_IHC_HEADNOTE":"(a) The Arbitration Act, 1940, Sec 34 --- Conditions required to be met for staying the proceedings in a suit. Test for determining whether an act is tantamount to a step in the proceedings or not.\r\n\r\nPLD 1981 SC 553 \u0026 1993 SCMR 866.\r\n\r\n(b) The Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, Order. XXXVII--- The Order does not bar a defendant from filing an application for the purposes of staying the proceedings under sec 34 of the Act of 1940. The provisions of Order XXXVII neither override nor expressly exclude the application of the Act of 1940. \r\n\r\n(c) Application under sec 34, The Arbitration Act, 1940--- In order to demonstrate an unequivocal intention not to waive the right to refer the matter for arbitration, the filing of an application under sec 34 of the Act of 1940 at the first instance would be crucial. It may well be filed independently and the same would also tantamount to seeking leave of the Court to appear without defending the suit. \r\n\r\nAppeal allowed. \r\n","O_UNDERSECTION":"1 \u0026 2---Arbitration Act (X of 1940), 3, 34 \u0026 39 Civil Procedure Code (V of 1908), 5 of the Limitation Act, 1908 ","O_REMARKS":"-","O_SUBJECT":"Civil Procedure Code, Arbitration ","APPL_IN":"** JGMNT","CASECODE":33703,"PRNTCASECODE":33703,"USERNAME":"UmerRasheedDar"},{"O_ID":1771,"TITLE":"Pakistan Oilfields Ltd.- VS -FOP and others","DDATE":"28-DEC-2015","ATTACHMENTS":"/attachments/judgements/WP-2333-2012_____________636971714907438971.pdf","ISLANDMARK":0,"O_AFR":1,"O_CITATION":"2016 PTD 824","PARTIES":"Pakistan Oilfields Ltd. It is not vested right---   Conditions required to be fulfilled for being eligible to be considered by the appointing authority in respect of the benefits of proforma promotion are i) the person must be a civil servant and, ii) he/ she was entitled to promotion from a particular date and, iii) was wrongfully prevented from rendering service to the Federation in a higher post for no fault of his/ her--- Prime Minister has laid down guidelines and prescribed a mechanism for processing claims for being considered for extending benefits through proforma promotion--- Recommendations thus made are placed before the appointing authority and the latter, after taking the same into consideration, decides whether or not to direct that the civil servant be paid the arrears of pay and allowance of a higher post though proforma promotion--- Reliance placed on PLD 1987 SC 172 \u0026 2005 SCMR 1742--- Observation, Proforma promotion essentially involves a change of benefits relating to a grade or post, and thus an elemtn of selection; therefore, as settled law, it cannot be earned automatically, rather it is within the exclusive jurisdiction of appointing authority--- Reliance on 1991 SCMR 1559 \u0026 PLD 2013 SC 255--- Intra Court Appeal was allowed ","O_UNDERSECTION":"Constitution of Pakistan, 1973------Art. 199--Law Reforms Ordinance, 1972, S. 3","O_REMARKS":"-","O_SUBJECT":"CONSTITUTION OF PAKISTAN 1973","APPL_IN":"** JGMNT","CASECODE":22368,"PRNTCASECODE":22368,"USERNAME":"UmerRasheedDar"},{"O_ID":1769,"TITLE":"Muhammad Rizwan- VS -The State","DDATE":"11-MAR-2016","ATTACHMENTS":"/attachments/judgements/WP-801-2011_____________636971710226650749.pdf","ISLANDMARK":0,"O_AFR":1,"O_CITATION":"2016 PCRLJ 998 Islamabad","PARTIES":"Muhammad Rizwan VS The State","CASENO":"Writ Petition-801-2011","CSNO":801,"BENCHNAME":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","CREATEDDATE":"\/Date(1561557485000)\/","AUTHOR_JUDGES":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","O_IHC_HEADNOTE":"Quashing of FIR---Principles and law as enunciated by the august Supreme Court examined and summarized---Principles and law relating to a private complaint and the extent of interference by a High Court. ","O_UNDERSECTION":"420, 467, 468 \u0026 471---Criminal Procedure Code (V of 1898), Ss. 154 \u0026 173, 200, 204 \u0026 561-A---Constitution of Pakistan, Art. 199","O_REMARKS":"-","O_SUBJECT":"QUASHMENT OF FIR","APPL_IN":"** JGMNT","CASECODE":5435,"PRNTCASECODE":15671,"USERNAME":"UmerRasheedDar"},{"O_ID":1768,"TITLE":"M/s IESCO through its Finance Director Mr. Najam Javed- VS -The Deputy Commissioner Inland Revenue, Audit II, LTU and 3 others","DDATE":"09-JUN-2016","ATTACHMENTS":"/attachments/judgements/STR-265-2011____________636971708350903455.pdf","ISLANDMARK":0,"O_AFR":1,"O_CITATION":"2016 PTD 2685","PARTIES":"M/s IESCO through its Finance Director Mr. Najam Javed VS The Deputy Commissioner Inland Revenue, Audit II, LTU \u0026 3 others","CASENO":"Sales Tax Reference-265-2011","CSNO":265,"BENCHNAME":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah, Former Honourable Mr. Justice Noor-Ul-Haq N. Qureshi","CREATEDDATE":"\/Date(1561557298000)\/","AUTHOR_JUDGES":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","O_IHC_HEADNOTE":"Question relating to status of supply of electricity by IESCO to the territory of AJK in context of levy and payment of sales tax--- Agreement between Governments of Pak and AJK and WAPDA in respect of raising Mangla Dam--- Notice to company for not paying deposited sales tax on the taxable supplies i.e. the supply of electricity made to AJK--- Reply of company based on two grounds: i) AJK was not a part of Pk ii) The agreement had the effect of exempting the Company from payment of sales tax on the supply of electricity made to AJK--- Examining the scheme of STA, 1990 and the status of AJK—Questions: whether the supply of electricity of AJK falls within the scope of export and is thus to be treated as goods exported and secondly, whether the agreement can be treated as an instrument or made the basis for the purposes of non payment of sales tax under the Act of 1990---  Interim Constitution of AJK--- Legislative wings--- AJK has special status but it is not a sovereign--- Next question: interpretation of the expression ‘goods exported’ in context of sec 4 of the Act of 1990--- Reliance on case law--- Words Import and export were given their ordinary meanings--- Defining EXPORT and EXPORTATION--- It is obvious that the expression ‘export’ \u0026 ‘import’ are complementary to each other--- AJK is not a country wherein goods are imported from Pak--- It is for this reason that the procedure prescribed for exporting goods from Pakistan is neither attracted in the case of goods sent to AJK nor are they dealt with as imports upon entry into the said territory--- Goods taken out of the territories specified in Art. (1) 2 of the Constitution and sent to AJK cannot be treated as ‘goods exported’ from Pak to another country--- Neither the sales tax on supplied of electricity made by the company to AJK were exempt nor could have been charged at the rate of zero percent as zero rated supplies.","O_UNDERSECTION":"4, 13, 1, 34 of the Act of 1990, under Article 1(2) of the Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan, 1973 ","O_REMARKS":"-","O_SUBJECT":"ADMINISTRATION OF JUSTICE, INTERPRETATION OF STATUTES, WORDS \u0026 PHRASES, TAX LAWS, Sales Tax ","APPL_IN":"** JGMNT","CASECODE":10918,"PRNTCASECODE":10918,"USERNAME":"UmerRasheedDar"},{"O_ID":1767,"TITLE":"Mustehkam Cement Ltd- VS -Commissioner Inland Revenue","DDATE":"29-MAR-2016","ATTACHMENTS":"/attachments/judgements/STR-184-2011__________636971706658144481.pdf","ISLANDMARK":0,"O_AFR":1,"O_CITATION":"2016 PTD 2853","PARTIES":"Mustehkam Cement Ltd VS Commissioner Inland Revenue","CASENO":"Sales Tax Reference-184-2011","CSNO":184,"BENCHNAME":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah, Former Honourable Mr. Justice Noor-Ul-Haq N. Qureshi","CREATEDDATE":"\/Date(1561557128000)\/","AUTHOR_JUDGES":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","O_IHC_HEADNOTE":"Sales Tax Act, 1990 --- Question of law proposed is stated to have arisen from the order passed by the Appellate Tribunal Inland Revenue--- Examining the scheme of Sales Tax Act, 1990. Secs. 3, 6, 7  --- Proviso of subsection (1) of section 7--- ‘Input tax’--- Sales Tax Act, 1990, sec. 10--- A plain reading of secs. 3, 6, 7 \u0026 10 conjunctively shows that inadmissible adjustment of input tax amounts to tax not levied or short levied--- A person claiming a refund of the input tax in excess of the output tax has to follow the prescribed procedure as provided under the Act, 1990 and the Rules made there under--- Reference made to Chapter –V of the Sales Tax Rules of 2006--- It is settled law that where the law requires an act to be done in a particular manner, it ought to be done in that manner alone, Reliance placed on PLD 2013 SC 255--- In a self-assessment scheme such a lapse on the part of the registered person is neither expected nor can the latter claim immunity from the consequences arising there from e.g. imposition of a penalty under sec. 33 or default surcharge under sec. 34 of the Act, 1990---  Sales Tax Act, 1990, sec. 33--- Filing of a return and an untrue or false statement made therein e.g. claiming inadmissible adjustment of input tax will attract clause 11 (a) of sec. 33---  Sales Tax Act, 1990, sec. 34--- The legislative intent is obvious from a plain reading of sec. 34. The legislature has described the categories of registered persons and the eventualities which would attract default surcharge under section 34---  ---  Sales Tax Act, 1990, sec. 36--- The crucial expression is tax levied or short levied--- Imposition, assessment as well as collection of a tax falls within the ambit of the expression ‘levy’--- There is no doubt that a case of inadmissible adjustment of input tax falls within the scope of sec. 36 and, therefore, the competent authority in the instant case was empowered to issue a show cause notice as contemplated therein--- Sales Tax (Refund of Excess Input Tax to the Manufacturers) Rules, 2005--- The Rules impose conditions and restrictions besides prescribing a procedure. Applicant had admittedly adjusted inadmissible input tax and had used invoices twice. It was, therefore, not a case covered under the Rules of 2005. ","O_UNDERSECTION":"3, 11 OF Sales Tax Act (VII of 1990)","O_REMARKS":"-","O_SUBJECT":"TAX LAWS","APPL_IN":"** Muhammad Abbasi","CASENO":"Murder Reference-6-2011","CSNO":6,"BENCHNAME":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Aamer Farooq, Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","CREATEDDATE":"\/Date(1561556921000)\/","AUTHOR_JUDGES":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","O_IHC_HEADNOTE":"Observations, when accused raises the plea of self-defence of his/ her person or property and wants to bring his/ her case within one of the exceptions, then the onus for establishing the plea lies upon him/ her --- Circumstantial evidence must be strong enough to award capital punishment --- It is settled law that the relationship of a witness by itself is not sufficient to discard the statement of an interested witness ---  Held, the prosecution had established the guilt of accused --- Murder reference was affirmed and conviction and sentence was confirmed.\r\nDefence side could not shatter the eye- testimony of wife of deceased, her testimony could not be set aside on ground of false implication due to corroboration of same through strong evidence, version that deceased was accompanied by someone inflicted wound on respondent is not accepted, promptness in registering FIR  ---  In case, an accused raises the plea of self-defence of his person or property and wants to bring his case within one of the exceptions, then the onus for establishing the plea lies upon him --- Circumstantial evidence must be strong enough to award capital punishment --- It is settled law that the relationship of a witness by itself is not sufficient to discard the statement of an interested witness ---  held, the prosecution established the guilt of Mohammad Abbassi --- Murder reference was affirmed and conviction and sentence was confirmed. ","O_UNDERSECTION":"302(b) \u0026 34 Penal Code (XLV of 1860), 121 OF Qanun-e-Shahadat (10 of 1984)","O_REMARKS":"-","O_SUBJECT":"PAKISTAN PENAL CODE (PPC), CRIMINAL TRIAL, MAXIMS, WORDS \u0026 PHRASES, QANUN-E-SHAHADAT","APPL_IN":"** JGMNT","CASECODE":14054,"PRNTCASECODE":11583,"USERNAME":"UmerRasheedDar"},{"O_ID":1765,"TITLE":"Malik Muhammad Rafique- VS -Mst. tanveer Jahan etc","DDATE":"26-DEC-2014","ATTACHMENTS":"/attachments/judgements/CR-06-2011______________636971702921937919.pdf","ISLANDMARK":0,"O_AFR":1,"O_CITATION":"2015 PLD 30 Islamabad","PARTIES":"Malik Muhammad Rafique VS Mst. tanveer Jahan etc","CASENO":"Civil Revision-6-2011","CSNO":6,"BENCHNAME":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","CREATEDDATE":"\/Date(1561556755000)\/","AUTHOR_JUDGES":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","O_IHC_HEADNOTE":"Compulsory DNA testing., Reliance on PLJ 2013 SC 107 ---Settled law consent of victim is necessary in cases of DNA testing otherwise compulsion may result into interference with liberty---  Petitioner has not been able to make out a strong prima facie case ---Petition dismissed. Qureshi","CREATEDDATE":"\/Date(1561555816000)\/","AUTHOR_JUDGES":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","O_IHC_HEADNOTE":"Appeals of respondent no. 1 had been allowed on ground that a refund could not be declined for the reason that it was hit by limitation. Appeals of department against allowing of appeals of respondent were filed and were subsequently dismissed. Income Tax Ordinance, 2001. sec. 221--- Question involved in the instant references essentially relates to the applicability of the limitation period prescribed under the section--- But no stretch of the imagination can section 221 be invoked for claiming a refund of an amount already deposited--- Reliance made on PLD 1998 SC 64 \u0026 2009 SCMR 1421--- It makes it obvious that if a person has wrongly or mistakenly deposited a tax or duty and did not raise any protest then he can only claim the refund within the time prescribed under the relevant provision, otherwise he would be stopped, and the principles of laches and waiver would also be attracted.--- It is settled law that delay defeats equity--- A person may have an enforceable right, but if he/ she fails to enforce the said right within the time stipulated by law, then the right becomes unenforceable. Reliance made on 2012 SCMR 280; 2004 SCMR 944; PLD 2010 SC 705 \u0026 2006 SCMR 631--- Proposed question was answered in negative.","O_UNDERSECTION":"118-C, 133, 122 \u0026 221, 80-D,  Income Tax Ordinance (XLIX of 2001)","O_REMARKS":"-","O_SUBJECT":"TAX LAWS","APPL_IN":"** JGMNT","CASECODE":4924,"PRNTCASECODE":4924,"USERNAME":"UmerRasheedDar"},{"O_ID":1763,"TITLE":"Akram Rashid- VS -Hamid Ali Khan","DDATE":"05-MAY-2015","ATTACHMENTS":"/attachments/judgements/CR-80-2010_____________636971686599473250.pdf","ISLANDMARK":0,"O_AFR":1,"O_CITATION":"2015 CLC 1290","PARTIES":"Akram Rashid VS Hamid Ali Khan","CASENO":"Civil Revision-80-2010","CSNO":80,"BENCHNAME":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","CREATEDDATE":"\/Date(1561555122000)\/","AUTHOR_JUDGES":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","O_IHC_HEADNOTE":"Principles relating to condonation of delay---whether Article 164 or Article 181 of the Limitation Act, 1908 was attracted in the facts and circumstances of the case, principles explained. \r\nSuit for declaration, permanent injunction and damages --- Application for condoning the delay was neither filed nor was reason for delay sufficient or satisfactory- Delay defeats equity and equity leans in favor of vigilant litigant--- Law of limitation, not a mere formality—Revision was dismissed. ","O_UNDERSECTION":"42 \u0026 54---Limitation Act (IX of 1908), Arts.164 \u0026 181---Civil Procedure Code (V of 1908), O.IX, R.13, Specific Relief Act (I of 1877), 3 of Limitation Act (IX of 1908)","O_REMARKS":"-","O_SUBJECT":"SPECIFIC RELIEF ACT, LIMITATION ACT","APPL_IN":"** JGMNT","CASECODE":19277,"PRNTCASECODE":19277,"USERNAME":"UmerRasheedDar"},{"O_ID":1762,"TITLE":"Commissioner of ltd","DDATE":"13-MAR-2018","ATTACHMENTS":"/attachments/judgements/ITR-71-2008_______________636971683231115334.pdf","ISLANDMARK":0,"O_AFR":1,"O_CITATION":"2018 PTD 1089","PARTIES":"Commissioner of Income Tax VS M/s Askari Commercial Bank ltd","CASENO":"Income Tax Reference-71-2008","CSNO":71,"BENCHNAME":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","CREATEDDATE":"\/Date(1561554786000)\/","AUTHOR_JUDGES":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","O_IHC_HEADNOTE":"Controversy between the department and the banks is whether the interest on Government securities is required to be offered for taxation on accrual or cash basis, treatment of nonperforming loans or bad debts, and taxation of loan which a Bank advances to its employees at concessional rates --- Taxation of interest on securities: Sections 17 (1) and 32 of ITO, 1979--- Term ‘receivable’ as defined in Black’s Law Dictionary, 8th edn--- ‘Receivable’ cannot be construed as involving actual receipt of income---  It is settled law of interpretation of a statute that provisions are required to be read in its context and in case of a taxing statute the Court has to look to the clear words ---It is also a settled rule of interpretation that in order to discover the legislative intent the statute has to be read as a whole--- Reliance on 2014 SCMR 1557: Supreme Court affirmed that the regularly adopted method of accounting by an assessee will inevitable determine the tax liability in case of interest on securities--- Term ‘Entitled’ as defined in various legal dictionaries--- ‘Entitled’ cannot be construed as having reference to actual receipt of income--- Settled principles of interpretation of a fiscal statute are that the provisions are required to be interpreted literally and equity or presumption are alien thereto; if a provision of a taxing statute can have two reasonable explanations then one which is favourable to the tax payer has to be accepted; any ambiguity is required to be resovled in favour of the tax payer. Likewise, redundancy cannot be attributed to the lawmaker--- Deliberation on other questions.","O_UNDERSECTION":"17(1), 23(1)(x), 24(i) \u0026 32 Income Tax Ordinance (XXXI of 1979), 2(12), 33, 34, \u0026 80(1)(b) of Income Tax Ordinance (XLIX of 2001)","O_REMARKS":"-","O_SUBJECT":"INTERPRETATION OF STATUTES, WORDS \u0026 PHRASES, TAX LAWS","APPL_IN":"** on 2011 PTD 690; Question relating to disputes and the determination of PCT headings have been considered and determined by the hierarchy of forums provided under the Customs Act of 1969--- Reliance placed on 2010 SCMR 241 and 2010 SCMR 164--- Scheme of Act of 1969--- It is settled law that that a schedule of a statute is subservient to the substantive provisions, and in case of conflict the latter shall prevail--- Reference: PLD 2007 SC 133 \u0026 1993 SCMR 338---The Status and Role of FBR; Reliance on 1993 SCMR 1232--- The scheme of Act of 1969 and ITO, 1979 in context of the role of the Board in relation to adjudication and the hierarchy of appellate forums is similar---In so far as quasi judicial functions are concerned the opinion, instructions or orders of the Board are not binding---Held, the adjudicating officer, as well as the appellate forums created under the Act of 1969, are competent and vested with the power and jurisdiction to resolve any controversy, whether factual or legal, relating to a dispute in the context of a PCT heading, independently. Question at serial number (iv) was given in negative. Minallah","CREATEDDATE":"\/Date(1560336593000)\/","AUTHOR_JUDGES":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","O_IHC_HEADNOTE":"(a) The Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, S. 115 \u0026 Order XLI, Rule 17 sub rule 1 \u0026 Rule 19 ---the Limitation Act, 1908, Arts 5 \u0026 168--- Civil Revision---   Dismissal of appeal for want of prosecution--- Dismissal of petition for re –admission/ restoration and the application for condoning the delay.\r\n(b) Duty of the Court to exercise care and precaution --- Dismissal of appeal--- Plight of overseas Pakistanis---Sole reliance on the professional conduct of their counsels--- Large number of litigation is pending in the courts wherein they have alleged to have been deprived of their properties through fraud and deceit. \r\n(c) The Limitation Act, 1908, Art. 168---Dismissal of appeal for the want of prosecution--- Nature and purpose/ object and significant of the law of limitation--- Law of limitation is crucial for ensuring certainty --- There is judicial consensus that the said law is to be strictly construed, and it’s nature is prescriptive and preventive--- A transaction based on fraud and forgery cannot be shielded on the touchstone of limitation--- Law of limitation favors the vigilant.\r\n(d) Question of limitation--- Mixed question of law and facts.\r\n(e) Fraud vitiates all proceedings.\r\n(f) Limitation against void order--- Limitation would not run against a void order.\r\n(g) Mistake advice of a counsel ---Limitation---  A mistake advice of a counsel would not be sufficient cause for condonation of delay and that the only remedy was to sue for damages for the loss suffered on account of the negligent act--- Ill advice could not be treated as a valid ground for extension of time of limitation--- Where the litigant and the counsel had acted with due care and caution and their conduct did not appear to be negligent then institution of an appeal in a wrong forum could constitution a sufficient cause within the meaning of section 5 of the Act of 1908. \r\n(h) Limitation period ---Condition of delay---  A party who seeks condontion is bound to show that the lis was prosecuted diligently and that there was no lapse or negligence on his/ her part or on the part of the agent and that negligence on the part of a counsel did not furnish a ground for condonation. \r\n(i) The Limitation Act, 1908, Art. 168--- Dismissal of regular first appeal --- Condonation of delay were dismissal order was void --- Re- admission--- Where requirements of due process had not been complied with, dismissal order is not sustainable and case fell within the ambit of Art. 168 of the Act of 1908. \r\n(g) Exercise of discretion--- In exercise of discretionary powers a court is not empowered to act arbitrarily or in a mechanical manner, rather it is circumscribed by the law, recognized norms of justice, fair play, equity, logic, rationality and reasonableness. \r\n(k) The Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, Order XLI, Rule 17 (1)--- Exercise of discretion--- Discretion vested in said provision cannot be exercised in a mechanical or perfunctory manner. There has to be application of mind and it ought to be manifested in order of dismissal. \r\nPetition allowed","O_UNDERSECTION":"3, 5 of the Limitation Act 1908, 115 of the CPC ","O_REMARKS":"-","O_SUBJECT":"Limitation Act, Discretion, Specific Relief Act","APPL_IN":"** JGMNT","CASECODE":55439,"PRNTCASECODE":55439,"USERNAME":"UmerRasheedDar"},{"O_ID":1542,"TITLE":"The State VS Muhammad Siddique","DDATE":"12-JUN-2019","ATTACHMENTS":"/attachments/judgements/C.S.R-2-2011____________________636959494251134227.pdf","ISLANDMARK":0,"O_AFR":1,"O_CITATION":"2019 PCRLJ 1600","PARTIES":"The State VS Muhammad Siddique","CASENO":"Capital Sentence Reference-2-2011","CSNO":2,"BENCHNAME":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","CREATEDDATE":"\/Date(1560335816000)\/","AUTHOR_JUDGES":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","O_IHC_HEADNOTE":"(a) The Anti-terrorism Act, 1997--- Mischief--- Law.\r\n\r\nShahbaz Khan alias Tippu and others versus Special Judge Anti- Terrorism Court No. 3, Lahore and others. Such disposal attracts the NAB Ordinance, section 9.--- The main issue involved is whether in facts of the petitions and circumstances land is being acquired for a public purpose--- State Largess--- CDA Ordinance, 1960—Master plan of Isb--- Task of Authority as given in Ordinance. The Authority was and continues to be under a statutory obligation to prepare Schemes and thereafter acquire land within the Specified Areas as has been hled by Supreme Court in CDA through Chairman and others versus Dr. Abdul Qadeer Khan and others [1999 SCMR 2636]- Sec 22 of the Ordinance, entire land shall be liable to acquisition at any time--- Rish involved in case of land in respect whereof notice under section 4 of the Act--- 1992 SCMR 993 (Haq Nawaz Khan and others versus Rab Nawaz and others ) --- Zoning Regulations--- A plain reading of regulations as a whole unambiguously shows that land is to be acquired and developed exclusively by the Authority and no other entity, not even another department or agency of the Government is empowered in this regard. --- Special law prevails over general law—Reliance placed on 2017 SCMR 999--- The provisions of the Ordinance, 1960 and the regulations made there under are of mandatory nature and are binding, so much so that a liberal construction thereof is not permissible. Reference in this regard is made to “In the matter of: Human Rights Cases Nos. 4668 of 2006, 1111 of 2007 and 15283- G of 2010 [PLD 2010 SC 759]” --- What is a public purpose?—Reliance placed on PLD 1983 SC 457 “The expression “public purpose” has no precise and rigid meaning except that it should have the criterion of benefit or advantage to the public as distinguished from the private interest of an individual”--- In the facts and circumstances, land is being acquired for the distribution of state largess amongst persons belonging to a certain service, institution or profession.—Expression Grace—State Largess involving substantial private financial benefit is available to only those who are selected by the Foundation. The selection has no rationa basis. There is nothing on record to justify the selection of the beneficiaries… It could not be explained why only those who work for the Government or Constitutional bodies are offered this benefit.--- Relinace placed on 1996 SCMR 1277—Unjust enrichment could be a factor negating public purpose--- the loss to the public and private financial gain for a few privileged individuals is obviously not a public purpose. Private financial gain is an antithesis of public purpse. Arbitrary and irrational distribution of land belonging to the people of Pakistan as a largess to a few individuals which benefits from financially and causes loss to the people of Pakistan by no stretch of the imagination comes within the ambit of expression “public purpose”","O_UNDERSECTION":"4, 42 Land Acquisition Act 1894, Capital Development Authority Ordinance (XXIII of 1960) 22, Ss. 4 \u0026 17","O_REMARKS":"-","O_SUBJECT":"LAND ACQUISITION ACT, CONSTITUTION OF PAKISTAN 1973, CAPITAL DEVELOPMENT AUTHORITY (CDA)","APPL_IN":"** others","DDATE":"25-NOV-2015","ATTACHMENTS":"/attachments/judgements/Intra Court Appeal-529-2014-ica-529-2014636552465968865503.pdf","ISLANDMARK":0,"O_AFR":1,"O_CITATION":"2016 PTD 1484","PARTIES":"PTCL VS FOP \u0026 others","CASENO":"Intra Court Appeal-529-2014","CSNO":529,"BENCHNAME":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","CREATEDDATE":"\/Date(1488440407000)\/","AUTHOR_JUDGES":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","O_IHC_HEADNOTE":"Maintainability of the intra court appeals on ground of the proviso to section 3 (2)  of Law Reforms Ordinance, 1972 where appeal arises from a constitutional petition wherein a notice/ show cause notice issued prior to passing an original order, wherein the relevant law provides for a right of appeal, review or revision against such order, has been challenged---    Observed, ICS were maintainable---Reliance placed on Pakistan Oilfields Ltd, Rawalpindi versus Province of Punjab, through Secretary Finance Department, Lahore and others [2010 SCMR 328] while interpreting the proviso to section 3 (2) has observed and held that an intra court Appeal would be maintainable if at the time of filing the constitutional petition under Art 199 of Constitution the remedy of appeal was not available. Reference also made on 2012 SCMR 114; 2001 PCrLJ 146 and also on 2013 PTD 1114--- Appeals annexed C—Observations, It is settled law that a harmonious interpretation be given to statutory provisions.--- the Ordinance of 2001--- Section 177 provides for the power  of audit and so does section 214-C--- Analyzing the several amendments made to section 177 from time to time ---the Commissioner to exercise powers envisaged under section 177 (2)--- assessment order under section 120--- Section 122 provides for the mechanism and the safeguard for amending an assessment order--- under subsection 4 clauses (i) to (iii) exist. Plain reading of sections 177 and 122 shows that the Commissioner is empowered to call for the record, documents etc of any tax payer for the purposes of audit and to amend or further amend the assessment order deemed to have been passed under section 120--- Inland Revenue Commissioner (1921)-- Principles of interpretation of fiscal statute---powers of the Commissioner under section 177 be construed or interpreted as being subservient to the powers of the Board under section 214- C--- Held, power of the Commissioner to call for records and conduct an audit under section 177 does not require any pre-selection process by the Board--- However, the commissioner has certain obligations while exercising powers under section 177 of the Ordinance of 2001--- Appeals allowed with the direction that the relevant Commissioners to afford an opportunity of hearing to the appellants before proceeding to conduct the audit pursuant to the notices--- other Appeals annexed as B under the Sales Act of 1990 ---- Direct or indirect taxes--- Act 1990 was enacted with the object to consolidate and amend the law relating to the levy of tax on sales, importation, exportation, production, manufacturing or consumption of goods--- Sec 25 empowers the commissioner to direct a person to produce the specified records, documents as and when required--- Power to select is vested in the Commissioner and the selection is to be made as and when the latter requires such a person to produce the record etc--- Commissioner’s obligations--- section 177 of the Ordinance 2001 would also be attracted when powers are exercised under section 25 of the Act, 1990---Routine audit and an audit--- It is statutory duty of the commissioner to select a person for audit and give reasons based on the principles --- and the Federal Excise Act of 2005---Sec. 46 empowers the officer of Inland Revenue authorized by the Board or the Commissioner to conduct audit  of the record and documents of any person registered under the Act once a year after giving advance notice in writing to such person.--- section 42-B provided for the power of the Board to select a person or classes of persons for audit of the record and documents through computer ballot- the power cannot be read in section 46 so as to interpret the exclusive and independent power conferred on the Commissioner as being subservient thereto --  Appeals allowed.","O_UNDERSECTION":"Ss.120 \u0026 177, 214-C Income Tax Ordinance (XLIX of 2001)","O_REMARKS":"-","O_SUBJECT":"INTERPRETATION OF STATUTES, TAX LAWS","APPL_IN":"** JGMNT","CASECODE":42228,"PRNTCASECODE":42228,"USERNAME":"zulqarnainshah"},{"O_ID":350,"TITLE":"PAEC Foundation Housing Scheme etc- VS -Sharf-ul-islam
