Pakistan","DDATE":"09-FEB-2017","ATTACHMENTS":"/attachments/judgements/I.C.A. 571-2012 Writ -ICANo.571of2012.TheCollegeofPhysicianandSurgeonsofPakistanvs.TheFOP,etc.636224200848620681.pdf","ISLANDMARK":0,"O_AFR":1,"O_CITATION":"2017 CLC 1306, 2017 PLJ 292 Islamabad","PARTIES":"Collage of Physician \u0026 Surgeons of Pakistan VS Federation of Pakistan","CASENO":"Intra Court Appeal-571-2012","CSNO":571,"BENCHNAME":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","CREATEDDATE":"\/Date(1486804789000)\/","AUTHOR_JUDGES":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","O_IHC_HEADNOTE":"Establishment of The College of Physician and Surgeons of Pakistan (CPSP) under the Pakistan Physicians and Surgeons Ordinance, 1962--- PMDC--- Constitution under Pakistan Medical \u0026 Dental Council Ordinance, 1962--- Mental and Dental Degrees Ordinance, 1982--- Establishment of The Shalamar Medical and Dental College --- The Hospital and the respondents/ consultants challenged the letter of the Council whereby they had been informed that the Hospital was not recognized for the post-graduate training program and that it had been granted recognition only for the purposes of house job training--- In the writ petition filed before single bench, the college was directed to initiate the process by recommending and forwarding to the Council, after fulfilling the formalities, a request for recognition of the Hospital--- Contention of the college has been that it was established under a statute which empowers them to grant accreditation and, therefore, it is not under an obligation to inform the Council or to seek its approval--- CPSP Ordinance, 1962; PMDC Ordinance, 1962, Third Schedule of the PMDC Ordinance, 1962, and the Schedule of the Ordinance, 1982--- Examining the relevant provisions of the three distinct statutes i.e. the CPSP Ordinance, 1962, the PMDC Ordinance, 1962, and the Ordinance, 1982--- THE CPSP Ordinance of 1962 has not defined the expression ‘accredit’ or ‘accreditation’ nor is it mentioned in the entire statute---- Black’s Law Dictionary --- Oxford English Dictionary--- The Chambers Dictionary, 10th edn--- There is no force in the argument advanced by the learned counsel for the College that the latter is not required to either inform or seek concurrence of the Council when a medical facility or a hospital is selected or ‘accredited’ by it--- All qualifications relating to medicine, whether at graduate or post graduate level, and whether in the nature of degrees, licenses or diplomas or in any other form, are regulated exclusively by and under the PMDC Ordinance 1962--- Almost all the facets of medical institutions, which trains for or grants or both trains for and grants degrees, diplomas or licenses in medicine, other than veterinary medicine, fall within the ambit and are regulated under the PMDC Ordinance of 1962--- The College has no power or jurisdiction to grant ‘accreditation’ to a hospital or medical facility and it would definitely not clothe such an entity with a legal status for the purposes of the PMDC Ordinance, 1962---  Approval or accreditation of a hospital in relation to a recognized additional medical qualification exclusively falls within the ambit of PMDC Ordinance, 1962--- The college is exclusively within the domain of the regulatory framework provided under the PMDC Ordinance, 1962--- it was decided that an accreditation of a hospital by the College for the purposes of a recognized additional medical qualification is without lawful authority and has no legal status, unless endorsed or approved under the PMDC Ordinance, 1962--- Regulatory role of the Council as laid down by the august Supreme Court--- Reliance placed on PLD 2007 SC 323--- The three statutes have distinct and separate objects and purposes and by no stretch of the imagination can the CPSP Ordinance, 1962, be construed as exempting the College from the provisions of the PMDC Ordinance of 1962 or that it is vested with the independent power to grant accreditation to a hospital without the sanction or approval of the Council --- it was held that there was no merit in ICA--- Council was expected that the Council established under the PMDC Ordinance, 1962 shall take appropriate and timely measures, in consultation with the College, to remedy the irregularities in respect of accreditation or approval of hospitals in connection with the training of students or other matters leading to the award of the recognized additional medical qualifications granted by the College and included in the third schedule--- Appeal disposed off","O_UNDERSECTION":"College of Physician and Surgeons of Pakistan Ordinance, 1982","O_REMARKS":"-","O_SUBJECT":"PMDC","APPL_IN":"** JGMNT","CASECODE":19888,"PRNTCASECODE":19888,"USERNAME":"zulqarnainshah"},{"O_ID":344,"TITLE":"Managing Director, Overseas Foundation of Paksitan VS Federation of Pakistan through secretary M/o JGMNT","CASECODE":4163,"PRNTCASECODE":4163,"USERNAME":"zulqarnainshah"},{"O_ID":343,"TITLE":"Mst. Naheed Khan VS Election Commission of Pakistan and others","DDATE":"06-FEB-2017","ATTACHMENTS":"/attachments/judgements/W.P. 2008-2013 Election Other-wp-2008-2013636220549377036905.pdf","ISLANDMARK":0,"O_AFR":1,"O_CITATION":"2019 CLC 938","PARTIES":"Mst. Naheed Khan VS Election Commission of Pakistan \u0026 others","CASENO":"Writ Petition-2008-2013","CSNO":2008,"BENCHNAME":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","CREATEDDATE":"\/Date(1486440486000)\/","AUTHOR_JUDGES":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","O_IHC_HEADNOTE":"Petitioner assailed order passed by Election Commission of Pakistan--- Petitioner had filed application under Political Parties Order, 2002 to seek registration of a political party in the name of ‘Pakistan Peoples Party’ ---Whereupon respondent no. 2 had also filed an application to seek interference of commission not to allow the petition--- An application had also been filed by the Chairperson and Secretary General of the Pakistan Peoples Party (Shaheed Bhutto) praying that the name of the latter be changed to ‘Pakistan Peoples Party’ and the election symbol of sword be allocated---Respondent no. 4 had also filed an application for restoration of the symbol of the ‘sword’--- Applications of petitioner and respondents no. 1 \u0026 2 were dismissed--- It would be beneficial to examine the relevant provisions of the Constitution and the Order of 2002--- Art- 17: Freedom of Assembly \u0026 Freedom of Association--- Political Party---- The object and purpose of enacting the Order of 2002--- Art 2 of the order defining a political party--- An association of citizens, or a combination or group of such associations, formed with a view to propagating or influencing political opinion and participating in elections for any elective public office, or for the membership of a legislative body--- Expression ‘election commission’--- Chapter II of the Order of 2002--- Modalities to formation, constitution, and membership of a political party--- Art 4 allowing a party to formulate constitution--- Art 5 entitles every citizen, not being in the service of Pakistan as of right to form or be a member of a party--- Art 11 relates to the elections within a party--- Arts 12 \u0026 13--- Submission of certificate of fulfilling conditions to the commission--- Art 14--- Election symbol--- Art 15--- Dissolution of a political party--- Art 16 – the effects thereof--- There is no formal registration of a political party--- Before allotting a symbol, the Commission essentially has to satisfy regarding three fundamental characteristics; identify and determine an association formed with a view to propagate or influence political opinion and participate in elections for any elective public office or for membership of a legislative body; secondly, that such an association or conglomerate has a distinct name and, lastly, that such an entity has complied with the mandatory pre conditions prescribed in the Order of 2002---  There is no cavil to the proposition that an association of citizens, which meets the definition of a ‘political party’ under Art. 2 (d) of the Order of 2002, cannot propose a name which is similar to an existing political party unless the latter has given its consent--- Only one entity would be eligible to be recognized as a political party with a distinct name--- Commission has to make two pivotal determinations: the association of citizens which meets the definition of a political party under Art. 2 (d), and the  distinctiveness of the name--- Both these determinations are within the exclusive domain of the Commission and as a general rule not open to judicial review while exercising jurisdiction under Art. 199, unless it can clearly be shown that the findings are perverse or fanciful--- It is settled law that disputed questions of fact cannot be resolved while exercising jurisdiction under Art. 199--- If petitioner had never been a member of the party namely ‘Pakistan Peoples Party (Parliamentarian)’ raised disputed questions of fact--- It is settled law the disputed questions of fact cannot be determined under Art. 199--- Reliance placed on PLD 1980 SC 139; 1980 SCMR 933;1981 SCMR 291; 1989 SCMR 918; PLD 1991 SC 476; 1993 SCMR 618; 2000 SCMR 718; 2012 SCMR 416; 2011 SCMR 1813--- Regulatory mechanism is exclusively within the domain of the learned Commission and the latter can remedy such defect in exercise of powers conferred under the Constitution, read with the provisions of the Order of 2002--- Petition was dismissed","O_UNDERSECTION":"Arts. 5(1) \u0026 5(3)---Constitution of Pakistan, Arts. 63(1)(k), 199 \u0026 260 Political Parties Order (20 of 2002)","O_REMARKS":"-","O_SUBJECT":"ELECTION","APPL_IN":"** JGMNT","CASECODE":27202,"PRNTCASECODE":27202,"USERNAME":"zulqarnainshah"},{"O_ID":340,"TITLE":"Syed Azhar Abbas Jaffri- VS -HBFC Workmen Nasreen Akhtar VS Learned Justice of Peace","CASENO":"Writ Petition-3186-2012","CSNO":3186,"BENCHNAME":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","CREATEDDATE":"\/Date(1455082002000)\/","AUTHOR_JUDGES":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","O_IHC_HEADNOTE":"Provisions of section 154 of CPC examined and extent in the light of the precedent law; scope and powers of justice of peace  as contemplated under the provisions of Cr.P.C.\r\nQuestions came before the Court for perusal were: whether the SHO is conferred with the power to undertake an inquiry before complying with the provisions of section 154 of CRPC secondly whether the said respondent could have refused to fulfill the obligations prescribed under the said section and secondly the scope and powers of the Justice of Peace.  Reliance of Court on Muhammad Bashir versus Station House Officer and others (PLD 2007 SC 539). There is no authority lies with SHO not to register the FIR on the ground that prior investigation in needed before the actual registering of the case, he must fulfill his duty --- Scope and powers of justice of peace as enshrined under the said section ie section 154 of CRPC – Justice of peace was only required to examine whether the information received disclosed a cognizable offence or not. --- Petition was allowed. ","O_UNDERSECTION":"Ss. 22-A, 22-B \u0026 154 Criminal Procedure Code (V of 1898)---S.154, ---Constitution of Pakistan, Art. 199","O_REMARKS":"-","O_SUBJECT":"CODE OF CRIMINAL PROCEDURE","APPL_IN":"** JGMNT","CASECODE":19335,"PRNTCASECODE":19335,"USERNAME":"zulqarnainshah"},{"O_ID":91,"TITLE":"M/s Arslan Poultry Pvt. Justice Athar Minallah","O_IHC_HEADNOTE":"Petitioners assailed order passed by in exercise of revisional powers under sec 64-A, The Cooperative Societies Act, 1925, along with letter issued for including the names of petitioners in FIR --- Questions: whether the orders by Respondents suffer jurisdictional errors or were passed by committing illegality, whether the conduct of the petitioners entitles them to discretionary relief under Art. 199 of The Constitution of Islamic Republic of Pakistan, 1973, and also whether the FIR and Inquiry Report have attained finality --- Mentioning of wrong legal provision will not affect the legality of order --- Order XXV, Supreme Court Rules, 1980 --- Orders by Human Rights Cell are only for purpose of satisfying whether the application fulfils the mandatory requirements for treating it as an application to be placed before Court under Art 184 (3) and that proceedings undertaken are not treated as proceedings under Art. 184 (3)---Observations, in several cases unwarranted and unauthorized references of courts is a major cause for denying parties of right to fair trial ---Adequate remedies available to the petitioners under sec 249-A and sec 265-K, CRPC --- Instant petition, devoid of merit, is not maintainable --- Frivolous Litigation, reliance on case law --- Unwarranted practice, of naming the Courts in complaints which are made part of FIR,  violates the fundamental rights.  ","O_UNDERSECTION":"Constitution of Pakistan, 1973------Art. 184(3)","O_REMARKS":"-","O_SUBJECT":"CONSTITUTION OF PAKISTAN 1973, CO-OPERATIVE SOCIETIES, ADMINISTRATION OF JUSTICE","APPL_IN":"** JGMNT","CASECODE":31676,"PRNTCASECODE":76249,"USERNAME":"zulqarnainshah"},{"O_ID":89,"TITLE":"M/s Eastern Testing Services Pvt. Criminal proceedings and adjudication distinct and separate. They can proceed simultaneously. Section 37-A interpreted. \r\nPetitioner is partnership firm while petitioners 2 \u0026 4 are partners --- Assailing the order and registration of FIR --- Contention of petitioner that respondents no. 2 and 4 did not have jurisdiction to proceed against the petitioner due to fact that petitioner is registered under sales tax Act, 1990 and that without determination of tax liability criminal proceedings could not be initiated --- And that the petitioners are being treated differently arising the concerns for Art 9 \u0026 25--- Contentions of respondents that search of premises of petitioner were made after due investigation by NAB and material so collected surely amounts to tax fraud, and that the jurisdiction of respondents is vested under section 37- A of the Act, 1990---Observations, two questions were before the Court that whether the FIR could have been registered when admittedly the petitioner is registered under the Sales Tax Act, 1990 and secondly , whether the petitioners have been treated differently. Jurisdiction of respondent no. 2 extends to whole of Pakistan while respondent no. 3 to “all persons or classes of persons carrying on business within the territorial jurisdiction of Regional Tax Officer. The Respondent no. 3 is clothed with the power and jurisdiction to exercise powers under section 37- A of the Act, 1990 and extends t all persons or classes of persons carrying on business within the territorial jurisdiction of the Regional Tax Officer, Islamabad. The jurisdiction of the respondents no. 2 to 4 is therefore established --- Act of 1990 is fiscal in nature and therefore ahs to be interpreted in the light of the principles enunciated in respect of interpretation of fiscal legislation, proceedings under section 37-A ibid shall be competend and within the jurisdiction of the officers authorized in this regard --- Also neither any discrimination has been caused to the petitioners --- Petition was dismissed.","O_UNDERSECTION":"Sales Tax Act, 1990------S. 37-A--Constitution of Pakistan, 1973, Art. 199","O_REMARKS":"-","O_SUBJECT":"TAX LAWS","APPL_IN":"** JGMNT","CASECODE":47379,"PRNTCASECODE":47379,"USERNAME":"zulqarnainshah"},{"O_ID":76,"TITLE":"Irfan Ahmad, etc- VS -FOP through Secretary M/o Science and Technology, etc","DDATE":"11-DEC-2015","ATTACHMENTS":"/attachments/judgements/Writ Petition-13-2015-W.P.No.13of2015,aftercorrection.635905414844731682.pdf","ISLANDMARK":0,"O_AFR":1,"O_CITATION":"Citation Awaited","PARTIES":"Irfan Ahmad, etc VS FOP through Secretary M/o Science and Technology, etc","CASENO":"Writ Petition-13-2015","CSNO":13,"BENCHNAME":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","CREATEDDATE":"\/Date(1454927012000)\/","AUTHOR_JUDGES":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","O_IHC_HEADNOTE":"Provisions of Pakistan Science Foundation Act, 1973 examined in the context of interference by the Federal Government in the affairs of Pakistan Science Foundation. ","O_UNDERSECTION":"20 Limitation Act (IX of 1908), Arbitration Act (X of 1940)","O_REMARKS":"-","O_SUBJECT":"Arbitration","APPL_IN":"** JGMNT","CASECODE":51034,"PRNTCASECODE":51034,"USERNAME":"zulqarnainshah"},{"O_ID":73,"TITLE":"PTCL- VS -PTA","DDATE":"13-APR-2015","ATTACHMENTS":"/attachments/judgements/First Appeal Against Order-25-2012-FAONo.25of2012.635905410342563774.pdf","ISLANDMARK":0,"O_AFR":1,"O_CITATION":"2015 PLD 184 Islamabad","PARTIES":"PTCL VS PTA","CASENO":"First Appeal Against Order-25-2012","CSNO":25,"BENCHNAME":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","CREATEDDATE":"\/Date(1454926562000)\/","AUTHOR_JUDGES":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","O_IHC_HEADNOTE":"Rules of interpretation --- No rule or regulation can be made inconsistent with parent statute and any such delegated legislation is void and inoperative to the extent of the inconsistency ---- Reliance on case law--- In order to discover intention of legislature, the statute has to be read as a whole --- Redundancy is to be avoided and an effort has to be made to read provisions harmoniously --- Pakistan Telecommunication Authority (Re-organization) Act, 1996 vested power in the Authority to levy fine under sec. 23 ibid. High Court, in exercise of appellate jurisdiction, set aside order passed by Pakistan Telecommunication Authority and remanded the matter to the Authority where show cause notice was deemed to be pending and mater was to be decided afresh in accordance with law --- Appeal allowed.\r\n","O_UNDERSECTION":"7(1), 23 Pakistan Telecommunication Authority (Re-organization) Act (XVII of 1996) S. 23---Pakistan Telecommunication Rules, 2000, R.9, 5(1)","O_REMARKS":"-","O_SUBJECT":"INTERPRETATION OF STATUTES, WORDS \u0026 PHRASES, PERMRA AND PTA CASES","APPL_IN":"** JGMNT","CASECODE":15871,"PRNTCASECODE":15871,"USERNAME":"zulqarnainshah"},{"O_ID":72,"TITLE":"Great Bear International Services Pvt. Ltd.- VS -PTA","DDATE":"06-FEB-2015","ATTACHMENTS":"/attachments/judgements/First Appeal Against Order-33-2012-FAONo33of2012635905408765245004.pdf","ISLANDMARK":0,"O_AFR":1,"O_CITATION":"2015 CLD 1721 Islamabad, 2015 PLJ 290 Islamabad","PARTIES":"Great Bear International Services Pvt. Ltd. VS PTA","CASENO":"First Appeal Against Order-33-2012","CSNO":33,"BENCHNAME":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","CREATEDDATE":"\/Date(1454926404000)\/","AUTHOR_JUDGES":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","O_IHC_HEADNOTE":"Telecommunication (Re-organization) Act, 1996 ---Radio Frequency Spectrum Fee and late payment of additional fee--- The fee will be paid even if the spectrum not used. If a valid Board Resolution and authorization is not appended with appeal, nor submitted within the limitation period provided by the statute, then an appeal cannot be treated as having been instituted by the juridical person within the period of limitation. Reliance placed on 2014 CLD 415.\r\nAppeal against the enforcement order --- License was rendered to appellant under the Pakistan Telecommunication (Re-organization) Act, 1996 --- Respondent issued show cause notice under section 23 of said Act to submit the annual audited accounts along with payment of annual regulatory dues for the year ending on 30-06-2011 and annual Radio Frequency Spectrum Fee along with late payment additional fee--- Enforcement order followed the notice --- Respondent contended, inter alia, that the appeal is not maintainable due to defect of Board Resolution appended with Memo of Appeal and it is settled law that in case of juridical person, the lis cannot be initiated without a Board Resolution ---  Three questions whether the spectrum fee is to be charged of paid only on its use; secondly whether late payment fee pursuant to clause of license is independent or subject to Regulations 2006 and weather the appeal is competent in lack of Board Resolution --- Observations, the spectrum is precious scarce resource owned by the State, and its allocation attracts the fee notwithstanding its use, reliance on case law decided by Apex Court --- it is settled law that liabilities under an instrument, being in the nature of a contract, cannot be avoided when it has been entered into voluntarily, and out of the free will of the parties thereto --- Order XXIX of CPC relates to suits by or against corporation, and rule 1 thereof prescribes the manner for the pleadings to be verifies and signed ---Reliance on Supreme Court decisions, to show that it is settled law that for a suit to be validly instituted, it must have been so instituted by a person authorized in this behalf, through a resolution passed in a meeting of BOD--- Reliance on case law--- a suit or legal proceedings invalidly or incompetently instituted in name of company cannot be treated as legal proceedings by that company. It may be added that since these are not valid proceedings instituted by the company, therefore, even a subsequent ratification would not cure the defect by making the proceedings valid and competent. The wisdom behind the principle that the defect is incurable seems to  be the intention to protect the juridical person on the one hand and on the other hand to safeguard any attempt to circumvent the period of limitation. It may be possible that the defect is pointed out before the expiry of the period of limitation thus enabling the juridical person to cure the defect by withdrawing the suit and filing it afresh, or filing it after the dismissal on the ground of it being invalid or incompetent ---Producing an extract of a Board of Resolution after the expiry of limitation period for filing an appeal would not cure the institution of the appeal on the analogy that such a defect cannot be cured even if subsequently ratified. Held, it is concluded that if a valid Board Resolution and authorization is not appended with appeal, nor submitted within the limitation period provided by the statute, then an appeal cannot be treated as having been instituted by the juridical person within the period of limitation. Reliance placed on 2014 CLD 415. Appeal stood dismissed. An application under order 39/CPC was also filed and same was dismissed through the impugned order ---Appellant contended that impugned order failed to consider that in case no injunctive order was passed irreparable damage/loss will be caused ---Respondents argued that it is well settled law that in case of an unconditional guarantee, injunctive orders are issued only in exceptional circumstances i.e fraud and irretrievable loss ---Observations, after analyzing the three main categories of guarantees i.e. Conditional guarantee, un-conditional guarantee, Mobilization guarantee, court observed that there is nothing on record or otherwise to suggest that the circumstances exist so as to satisfy the test of ‘irretrievable harm or injustice’. Therefore appeal fails and is dismissed for granting an injunction and restraining the encashment of an irrevocable and unconditional guarantee, except in the exceptional circumstances would amount to changing the nature of the agreed terms and conditions of the guarantee, and frustrating the intention of the parties.     ","O_UNDERSECTION":"74, 126, 128 of Contract Act (IX of 1872), 104 of Civil Procedure Code (V of 1908)","O_REMARKS":"-","O_SUBJECT":"CONTRACT ACT, INTERPRETATION OF STATUTES","APPL_IN":"** JGMNT","CASECODE":43222,"PRNTCASECODE":43222,"USERNAME":"zulqarnainshah"},{"O_ID":70,"TITLE":"Imperial Electric Company Pvt Ltd- VS -Zhongxing Telecom Pakistan Pvt Ltd, etc","DDATE":"17-NOV-2015","ATTACHMENTS":"/attachments/judgements/Civil Suit-61-2014-CSNo61of2014635905406508233039.pdf","ISLANDMARK":0,"O_AFR":1,"O_CITATION":"2019 CLD 609","PARTIES":"Imperial Electric Company Pvt Ltd VS Zhongxing Telecom Pakistan Pvt Ltd, etc","CASENO":"Civil Suit-61-2014","CSNO":61,"BENCHNAME":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","CREATEDDATE":"\/Date(1454926179000)\/","AUTHOR_JUDGES":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","O_IHC_HEADNOTE":"Arbitration matter--- Parties may even agree to name an authority or person from amongst their own officers/ officials, and this would not render such arbitration agreement illegal or against public policy, as held by Supreme Court in PLD 2003 SC 808--- Characteristics of an arbitrator: PLD 1971 SC 516--- Difference between Arbitration and mediation--- A clause if not mentioning expression ‘arbitrator’ and ‘arbitration’ was held to be not bad [PLD  1956 (WP) Lahore 442;  AIR (34) 1947 Lahore 215; 1988 MLD 1131; 1990 MLD 857].    ","O_UNDERSECTION":"2(a), 34 Arbitration Act (X of 1940)","O_REMARKS":"-","O_SUBJECT":"Arbitration ","APPL_IN":"** JGMNT","CASECODE":42397,"PRNTCASECODE":42397,"USERNAME":"zulqarnainshah"},{"O_ID":69,"TITLE":"Abid Assoicates Agencies- VS -AREVA","DDATE":"28-MAY-2015","ATTACHMENTS":"/attachments/judgements/Civil Suit-33-2011-CSNo33of2011635905397788597724.pdf","ISLANDMARK":0,"O_AFR":1,"O_CITATION":"2015 MLD 1646 Islamabad","PARTIES":"Abid Assoicates Agencies VS AREVA","CASENO":"Civil Suit-33-2011","CSNO":33,"BENCHNAME":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","CREATEDDATE":"\/Date(1454925307000)\/","AUTHOR_JUDGES":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","O_IHC_HEADNOTE":"Application under section 34 of Arbitration Act, 1940 for stay was amended by addition of Section 4 (1) of the Recognition and Enforcement (Arbitration Agreements and Foreign Arbitral Awards) Act, 2011 --- Question before  Court was whether under section 4 (1) of the Act, 2011, the proceedings in the suit should be stayed and whether the plaintiff has made out a case for invoking the exceptions as stipulated in subsection 2 of section 4 --- Expression “void”, “inoperative” or “incapable” of being performed elaborated. \r\n--- Partial award had already been announced by ICC. Observations, Arbitration Agreement attracts the standard principles of contract law, therefore, as a corollary ‘null and void’, inoperative or ‘incapable of being performed’  will also have to be construed and interpreted accordingly.--- three stage enquiry to determine the governing law of the arbitration agreement, Reliance placed on Sulamerica CIA Nacional De Seguro SA versus ENSEA ENGENHARIA SA (2012) ECWA Civ 638 --- I ) Arbitraion agreement 2) Whether disputes covered by the agreement 3) proper law governing the agreement and if the agreement has become null, void, inoperative or incapable of being performed. Observations, Plaintiffs/ respondents are bound by commitment under Article 12 of Agreement, reliance placed on Hitachi Limited versus Rupali Polyster (1998 SCMR 1618). Held application is allowed and the proceedings in the suit are stayed. Justice Athar Minallah","O_IHC_HEADNOTE":"In terms of Art 38,Qanoon-e-Shahadat Order, admission of an accused before police cannot be used as evidence against the co-accused, only section 420/PPC seems attracted, whether he is involved in other cases involves further probe --- Bail in non-prohibitory clause, principles elucidated in 2009 SCMR 1488 ---Reliance placed on other decisions of Apex Court----Petitioner entitled to bail.\r\nSections 420, 468, 471, 467, 419 \u0026 34/PPC --- Petitioner contended that he was not nominated in FIR but was implicated on statement of arrested accused --- Observations, only material implicating the petition is statement of the co –accused, it was held in 2013 SCMR 669 that in terms of Art 38/ Qanoon-e-Shahadat Order, admission of an accused before police cannot be used as evidence against the co-accused, only section 420/PPC seems attracted, whether he is involved in other cases involves further probe ---His continued custody will not bring any advantage --- Bail in non-prohibitory clause principles as elucidated in 2009 SCMR 1488 ---Reliance placed on other decisions of Apex Court----Petitioner entitled to bail. Ltd.- VS -OOREDOO Q.S.C and 3 others","DDATE":"04-JUN-2015","ATTACHMENTS":"/attachments/judgements/Civil Suit-63-2014-C.S.63of2014.635905384353099464.pdf","ISLANDMARK":0,"O_AFR":1,"O_CITATION":"2019 CLC 887","PARTIES":"Telecom Services \u0026 Consultations Pvt. Ltd. VS OOREDOO Q.S.C \u0026 3 others","CASENO":"Civil Suit-63-2014","CSNO":63,"BENCHNAME":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","CREATEDDATE":"\/Date(1454923963000)\/","AUTHOR_JUDGES":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","O_IHC_HEADNOTE":"Brief survey of approach of courts in various jurisdictions in the context of forum selection clauses ---- Traditional approach of ‘forum non conveniens’ in English laws of conflict --- Principles---Upholding the sanctity of a contractual bargain is pivotal--- Courts will give effect to exclusive jurisdiction clauses unless the plaintiff is able to discharge the heavy burden of satisfying the Court that circumstances exist, not foreseen at the time of execution of the contract containing such a clause, the proceedings be allowed in a non contractual forum. --- Effect of section 28 of the Contract Act, 1872, it is by now settled law that the said provisions do not create a bar in enforcing forum selection clauses mutually agreed between the parties, Reliance on case- law.\r\nApplication filed under Order VII Rule 10 with section 151 of CPC, 1908--- The respondent/ plaintiff has claimed certain amounts as expenditure incurred due to its participation in the bidding process, and in addition has also claimed damages for breach --- Question before Court was whether this Court should proceed in the matter or give effect to the above mentioned forum selection clause? ---- Observations, forum selection clause or having option to make a choice of jurisdiction, have not only become common but favorably acknowledged by courts in almost every jurisdiction --- Forum selection clause may fall in different categories, depending on the intention of the parties --- Broadly, it is of two types: exclusive or non- exclusive. The former requires that disputes arising under the  contract, or in connection therewith, shall be taken exclusively to the Court specified in the clause, while that is not the case with the latter----- Brief survey of approach of courts in various jurisdictions in the context of forum selection clauses ---- Traditional approach of ‘forum non conveniens’ in English laws of conflict --- Principles--- US Supreme Court gave effect to a jurisdiction clause, and recognized the clause giving jurisdiction to a Court outside the US --- Also PLD 1920 SC 373 \u0026 1998 SCMR 1618  ---  Upholding the sanctity of a contractual bargain is pivotal--- Courts will give effect to exclusive jurisdiction clauses unless the plaintiff is able to discharge the heavy burden of satisfying the Court that circumstances exist, not foreseen at the time of execution of the contract containing such a clause, the proceedings be allowed in a non contractual forum. --- Effect of section 28 of the Contract Act, 1872, it is by now settled law that the said provisions do not create a bar in enforcing forum selection clauses mutually agreed between the parties, Reliance on case- law --- In the present case, the plain language of ‘forum selection clause’ is precise, unambiguous and clear, and there is no doubt that the parties had intended the jurisdiction of the English Courts to be ‘exclusive’--- Held, it the circumstances it would be just and porper to give effect to the exclusive clause contained in the respective process letters--- Application is allowed.  ","O_UNDERSECTION":"S. 28---Civil Procedure Code (V of 1908), O. VII, R. 10 Contract Act (IX of 1872)","O_REMARKS":"-","O_SUBJECT":"Contract Act, Recovery","APPL_IN":"** etc","DDATE":"13-MAR-2015","ATTACHMENTS":"/attachments/judgements/Civil Revision-1192-2010-C.R.No.1192of2010635905382729444078.pdf","ISLANDMARK":0,"O_AFR":1,"O_CITATION":"2015 CLC 1568, 2012 CLC 1088","PARTIES":"Mian Muhammad Ajmal VS Syed Nazar Hussain Shah Bukhari etc","CASENO":"Civil Revision-1192-2010","CSNO":1192,"BENCHNAME":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","CREATEDDATE":"\/Date(1454923801000)\/","AUTHOR_JUDGES":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","O_IHC_HEADNOTE":"Scope of jurisdiction of a Court under section 115 of CPC explained---Whether a Court can go beyond the decree---the remedy in a case where the decree does not reflect the terms of settlement or is not inconformity with the judgment. \r\nSale agreement with deceased --- Specific relief was claimed --- Compromise entered --- petitioner filed objection petition on execution proceedings --- Objection Petition dismissed --- Against this petitioner filed appeal later on dismissed--- on revision, petition was decided though Supreme Court remanded case back to this Court for decision afresh --- Objection from petitioner that decree never contained essential features of agreement --- Respondents stressed that Executing court cannot go beyond the decree as the scope of section 47 of CPC limits the powers of executing court---  Also scope of the jurisdiction of this court under section 115 of CPC is limited according to contentions of the respondents --- Observations, interpretation of section 115 of CPC reliance on PLD 1983 SC 53 --- Unless the case was not covered under section 115 of CPC and the High Court was satisfied in this regard, then in such a case no power could be exercised to interfere simply because it differs, however profoundly, with the conclusions of the subordinate Court regarding questions of law and facts. Principles laid down by Apex Court while interpreting section 115 --- Also reliance on PLD 2015 SC 137 ---  Once the case is covered under section 115 of CPC, then approaching the Court for the redressal of a grievance is not a mere privilege but a valuable right of an aggrieved party --- Hled, this Court has no unfettered poers under section 115 of the CPC --- Section 47 + Order XXI of CPC governs the powers and procedure for execution of decrees and orders --- What if decree does not contain the contents of agreements--- Reliance on PLD 1963 SC 265 --- August Supreme Court observed that two remedies were available concurrently i.e filing a subsequent suit, provided the subject matter of the previous suit was different and distint, or to amend the decree under section 152 and also that the remedy under the said section could also be provided suo moto by the competent court by amending the decree, so as to make it conform to the judgment. Precedent shows that an executing court cannot extend its jurisdiction to go behind the decree and question its correctness --- Two exception to the rule are when the decree is silent regarding what property was the subject matter of execution, (2003 SCMR 1202) and the executability of a decree can be questioned by the executing court if it is satisfied that a) the decree is a nullity in the eyes of the law, b) it has been passed by a Court having no jurisdiction c) the execution  of the decree will not infringe the legal rights of the decree holder, if refused to be executed or the decree has been passed in violation of any provision of law (PLD 2001 SC 131) --- Hled, the objection petition filed by the petitioner raised matters which are neither expressed in the decree, nor can be construed as part thereof. In case the petitioner was aggrieved on ground that the decree had not been drawn up according to the compromise/ settlement or that there was some other error, then an appeal was preferred by the petitioner ---- Since the executing court could neither modify nor take into consideration documents or the agreement, which is not reflected or incorporated in the decree, petitioner has not been able to satisfy this Court that interference is required nor a case is made out so as to exercise jurisdiction under section 115 of CPC. Where a fresh suit has been filed by petitioner, while an application under section 152 of CPC is also pending before trial court for amendment of the decree, petition is not maintainable. ","O_UNDERSECTION":"47, 152 \u0026 2(2) Civil Procedure Code (V of 1908)","O_REMARKS":"-","O_SUBJECT":"CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE, SPECIFIC RELIEF ACT","APPL_IN":"** JGMNT","CASECODE":4117,"PRNTCASECODE":4117,"USERNAME":"zulqarnainshah"},{"O_ID":62,"TITLE":"Ansar Mehmood- VS -Jamshed Ahmed Mustafa etc","DDATE":"25-AUG-2014","ATTACHMENTS":"/attachments/judgements/Civil Revision-198-2014-C.R.NO.198of2014635905380886663912.pdf","ISLANDMARK":0,"O_AFR":1,"O_CITATION":"2015 PLD 1 ISLAMABAD, 2014 PLJ 404 Islamabad","PARTIES":"Ansar Mehmood VS Jamshed Ahmed Mustafa etc","CASENO":"Civil Revision-198-2014","CSNO":198,"BENCHNAME":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","CREATEDDATE":"\/Date(1454923617000)\/","AUTHOR_JUDGES":"Former Honourable Chief Justice Mr. Justice Athar Minallah","O_IHC_HEADNOTE":"Property Dispute ---Civil revision under section 115 CPC against allowing appeal under section 152 CPC ---Petition admitted.","O_UNDERSECTION":"S.152, C.P.C.---Civil Procedure Code (V of 1908)---S. 152 \u0026 O.
